Summary: Crash on MATLAB startup in x86CodeGenerator::x86CodeGenerator
Description: MATLAB may crash on startup or shortly thereafter when run on operating systems that have some form of memory protection for viruses (Windows XP or newer versions of Linux, for example).  This is because the JIT may generate code in sections of memory that have not been properly prepared by the operating system to be treated as executable code pages.
Workaround: Start up MATLAB with the -nojit option.

Summary: Code generation SegV for model with merge blocks and custom data objects
Description: Code generation for an ERT-based Simulink model can fail with a segmentation violation if all of the following conditions exist: The model associates a signal with a custom data object The signal is connected to the output of a merge block The merge block is at the end of a chain of one or more merge blocks

Summary: Mac OS 10.4.4: MATLAB fails to start after updating to 10.4.4.
Description: After updating Mac OS to version 10.4.4, MATLAB might fail to start and reports a segmentation violation. The Java VM is one cause of this problem. To determine whether the Java VM is causing this problem on your system, try starting MATLAB without the Java VM: Start X11 xterm (X11 is in /Applications/Utilities). Type the following command, substituting matlabroot with the path to the MATLAB application folder: matlabroot /bin/matlab -nojvm  If MATLAB successfully starts without the Java VM in xterm, the problem with startup is with the Java VM.
Workaround: If the problem is with the Java VM, work around the problem by reinstalling the Java VM.Use the most recent version of the Java VM, version 1.4.2_09 or later, which you can find by searching the Apple support Web site, www.apple.com/support.

Summary: MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine worker hangs while running a task on Macintosh
Description: When running on a Macintosh, a MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine worker begins running a task, but never seems to finish it.  The mdce-service  log file contains the following message:*** ATTENTION ***Please Attach Debugger: No Autorelease pool existsPress any key to continue.
Workaround: Check the version of Java running on the Macintosh by running java -version from a shell command line.If the Java version number is lower than 1.4.2_09, upgrade Java on the worker node to this version or later.

Summary: Issues with Autothreshold block and code generation with Real-Time Workshop
Description: You might get errors when you try to generate code from the model using Real-Time Workshop if the following two conditions apply:the model contains an Autothreshold block the model uses fixed-point data types

Summary: 2-D Mean block might produce incorrect results
Description: If the input is an unsigned fixed-point or integer data type and the output data type is not the same as the input data type, the 2-D Mean block outputs incorrect results.

Summary: Running CAGE optimizations with display option 'iter' causes MATLAB to freeze
Description: Setting the CAGE optimization option 'Display' to 'iter' causes the output of the optimization algorithm to be printed to the MATLAB command window.  This setting causes MATLAB to stop responding.
Workaround: If you are using Version 2.1.2, please read the following.This problem can be fixed by updating a CAGE optimization function as follows: Close MATLAB Rename the file $matlabroot/toolbox/mbc/mbcdata/mbcOSfmincon.m to $matlabroot/toolbox/mbc/mbcdata/mbcOSfmincon_old.m Download the file mbcOSfmincon.m and place it in the directory $matlabroot/toolbox/mbc/mbcdataRestart MATLAB

Summary: Issues with the Block Processing block help
Description: If you try to access help for the Block Processing block using the block dialog box, the wrong help page opens in the Help browser.

Summary: Problem writing large Excel file on Windows XP
Description: An error occurs when writing out a certain size of file in MATLAB 7.0.4 running on Windows XP SP2. The text of the error message appears as shown here:??? Invoke Error, Dispatch Exception: Source: Microsoft Office Excel Description: Impossible to access 'Crane.xls'. Help File: D:\Applications\MSOffice\OFFICE11\1036\xlmain11.chm Help Context ID: 0
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you select Edit->Copy Figure in a Figure window.
Description: If you close a figure window while the figure is being copied using the Edit  menu's Copy Figure  option, MATLAB crashes.
Workaround: Avoid closing the figure until the Copy Figure operation is complete.

Summary: Startup Fails and Generates Segmentation Violation
Description: MATLAB sometimes fails to start and generates a segmentation violation that includes the following in the stack trace:   [0] libmwm_interpreter.so:x86CodeGenerator::MeasureProfilerOverhead()
Workaround: Start MATLAB with the -nojit startup option.

Summary: MATLAB client computer not recognized by its short hostname
Description: The MATLAB client exports itself on its short hostname by default.The job manager may not be able to call back to the client using this short name.  Also, this short hostname may be different from the name by which the client computer is known on the network.
Workaround: To work around this problem,Close and restart the MATLAB session on the client computer. Type in the following before running any DCT code (including findResource).java.lang.System.setProperty('java.rmi.server.hostname', 'machine32.mydomain.com');orjava.lang.System.setProperty('java.rmi.server.hostname', 'fdm4');(Substitute the appropriate name of your machine for machine32.mydomain.com or fdm4.)This forces the MATLAB client to use the name by which the job manager can find it.Stop and restart the mdce service on the job manager computer to make sure that the job manager uses the new name.

Summary: C24x ADC conversion completion checks wrong bit
Description: The code generated by the C24x ADC block to check for the end of conversion is incorrect. The block generates:while((MMREGS[ADCTRL2]&=0x0010>>4)){} orwhile((MMREGS[ADCTRL2]&=0x1000>>12)){}  The code we should generate:while(MMREGS[ADCTRL2]&0x0010){} orwhile(MMREGS[ADCTRL2]&0x1000){}  Because of this, there is no guarantee that the conversion will be completed when the results are read.
Workaround: The attached TLC file contains the fix for R14SP2 release. Place this file in the toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks\tlc_c folder in your MATLAB root folder. Similar modification will work for other releases.

Summary: Referencing a continuous model can incorrectly produce a step-size mismatch error
Description: Simulink incorrectly reports a step-size mismatch error when a fixed-step model references a purely continuous model having a different fixed step size. Simulink should report the error only if the referenced model contains both discrete and continuous tasks.

Summary: Diagnostic dialog boxes appear when the Lookup Table Editor displays data of any type other than double
Description: Diagnostic dialog boxes appear when the Lookup Table (LUT) Editor renders a table containing data of any data type other than double . Depending on what you've done to that point, you get a warning and/or an error pop-up: Lookup Table Editor Warning: 'Table data and/or breakpoint data havechanged.  Do you want to update the block data? {yes, no}.   Whether you select yes or no, the following message appears: Error Message: 'Entry is not an valid finite number. Change has been voided. {OK}.  After you respond, the LUT Editor renders that table and you can modify it. But if you switch to a table containing data of any other type (including double ), the diagnostic messages appear again.

Summary: Lookup Table editor displays incorrect data for Lookup Table (2D) block
Description: The Lookup Table editor initially displays the data for a Lookup Table (2D) block incorrectly.

Summary: Double-clicking on mimimized scope block hangs MATLAB
Description: Minimizing an open Simulink scope display window and then double-clicking on its corresponding scope block hangs MATLAB.
Workaround: Use the Windows toolbar to restore (unminimize) the scope display instead of double-clicking on the scope block.

Summary: Lookup Table Editor starts improperly from Lookup Table (2D) block
Description: If you select the Edit  button from a Lookup Table (2D) block in a model with nested hierarchy (sf_car , for example), the Lookup Table Editor fails to display the table associated with the block.

Summary: Asynchronous functions: absolute time not generated correctly when nested in function call or triggered subsystem
Description: If all the following conditions exist in a model, generated code fails to compile due to the error struct "" has no field "clockTick#" , where # can be any number:  A block in an asynchronous function call subsystem must use absolute time.  The subsystem is inside another function-call or triggered subsystem.  The subsystem's sample time is different than the sample time of the parent subsystem.
Workaround: If you are using R14SP2, use attached file to replacematlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/rtmdllib.tlcReplace the file matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\rtmdllib.tlc as follows:  Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt. Quit MATLAB. Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\rtmdllib.tlc to matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\rtmdllib.tlc.old. Download the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Using Stateflow charts in configurable subsystems causes spurious corruption repairs
Description: Models with configurable subsystems that contain Stateflow charts cause the Stateflow loader to erroneously flag a model corruption. When the model is loaded, a subsystem named ###Recovered Stateflow Charts###  is created at the top level of the model. This subsystem contains duplicate copies of the charts in the configurable subsystem. Even after deleting this subsystem and resaving the model, the subsystem is recreated every time you load the model.
Workaround: There is a workaround for the R14SP2 release only. Follow these steps:Copy the attached file slsf.m to the following directory:toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/private Restart MATLAB and open the affected model.If you see the red-colored subsystem named ###Recovered Stateflow Charts### at the top level of the model, delete it and resave the model. You will now be able to load and save this model without recreating the ###Recovered Stateflow Charts### subsystem.

Summary: Logical operations on double values might yield wrong results in Stateflow
Description: When debugging is enabled, the use of logical operations (such as  a && b ) on data types, such as double or float, causes wrong code to be generated. Because of truncation effects, enabling debugging causes the condition to be false when it is actually true.  For example, 1.0  is sometimes represented at runtime as 0.99999999  which is truncated to yield 0  on an integer cast.
Workaround: As a workaround, for previous releases, change the types of these variables to integer or boolean. You can also use an explicit !=0 to avoid this problem. For example:(a!=0) && (b!=0)

Summary: Improper global destructor operation with shared libraries on 32-bit Linux
Description: Some components of the GCC runtime shipped with MATLAB that rely on position-independent code to function correctly are malformed.  The result is that cleanups tied to the .fini  section (notably, global destructors for C++ objects) are invoked at the wrong time. This causes global destructors within MATLAB or within a MEX module to be invoked when a shared library originally loaded via dlopen  is unloaded by dlclose .  This problem typically appears only on systems running glibc version 2.3.x  or later. An unrelated bug in the implementation of __cxa_finalize  in glibc 2.2.x  masks the problem.

Summary: Stateflow Debugger shows erroneous results for matrix values
Description: The Stateflow Debugger Browse data  option incorrectly displays the local data of matrix. In particular, it displays the transpose of the matrix instead of the correct orientation.

Summary: Why does the label for Video Input block report incorrect hardware information?
Description: If I change the hardware connected to my computer while the Image Acquisition block library is open, incorrect information is sometimes reported on the Video Input block's label, even if I call imaqreset . For example, if I drag the Video Input block from the library into a model when no hardware is connected to my PC, the block has the label "No Device," which is correct since no devices are connected to the PC. If I attach a device to the PC and call imaqreset , the device is recognized by imaqhwinfo  but any subsequent Video Input blocks that I add to my model from the library still display the "No Device" label. Similarly, if I drag the Video Input block from the library into a model when hardware is connected to my PC, the block's label correctly indicates the device name and format. If I disconnect the device from the PC and call imaqreset  the device is no longer returned by imaqhwinfo , but any subsequent Video Input blocks I add to my model from the library still display the disconnected device's name and format on the block's label.

Summary: Code generation failure in models with both SimDriveline and SimMechanics blocks
Description: Code generation from a model that contains both SimDriveline and SimMechanics blocks fails at the linking stage because of multiply-defined symbols. The pre-compiled code libraries that ship with each product both contain the utility functions with the same name.

Summary: Accessing global data stores at different rates can cause invalid errors
Description: Simulink can incorrectly report global data store access errors in the following cases: The Multitask data store  diagnostic is set to none  or warning  and the readers and writers of a global data store execute in different tasks. The readers and writers of a given global data store execute in a single task at the continuous rate and the fastest discrete rate.

Summary: Rate Transition block: model builds fail for slow-to-fast rate transfers with non-zero sample time offset
Description: When a model includes a Rate Transition block for which the input and output rates use the same sample time, but different sample time offsets, the model fails to build due to the following TLC error: Error: File: D:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 173 Column: 8   %exit directive:  Real-Time Workshop Fatal in block: "/Rate Transition", block type   "RateTransition": Invalid rate transition type
Workaround: If you are using R14SP2, replace matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/blocks/ratetransition.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\ratetransition.tlc tomatlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\ratetransition.tlc.old.Download the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocksRestart MATLAB.

Summary: The operspec function used with Discrete-Time Integrator blocks can return an error in some cases
Description: When the operspec  function is used to compute an operating point specification object for a model using the following command spec = operspec('model') and the model contains Discrete-Time Integrator blocks with the Limit output  checkbox selected and the Initial condition source  set to external , the following error is returned: ??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.Error in ==> operspec at 114op = op.update;
Workaround: To fix this problem, save the attached file update.m to the $matlabroot\toolbox\shared\slcontrollib\@opcond\@OperatingSpec\ directory on your system. Before saving the attached file, you might want to create a backup of the old update.m file by renaming it update_old.m.

Summary: Hardware triggered continuous acquisition stops on Measurement Computing hardware
Description: If you set up a hardware triggered acquisition on Measurement Computing hardware with SamplesPerTrigger  set to Inf , the acquisition stops without acquiring data when the hardware trigger occurs.  For instance,ai = analoginput('mcc',0); % change the device ID to your device IDaddchannel(ai,0);ai.TriggerType = 'hwDigital';ai.SamplesPerTrigger = Inf;start(ai); will stop prematurely without collecting any data.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can specify a large number of samples for the device to acquire, to simulate a continuous acquisition.ai = analoginput('mcc',0); % change the device ID to your device IDaddchannel(ai,0);ai.TriggerType = 'hwDigital';ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 5000; % or pick a larger value if appropriate.start(ai); This example will collect 5,000 points after the hardware trigger.  You can stop the acquisition early by using the stop command.

Summary: Hyphen is not supported in Host name field of VHDL Cosimulation Block
Description: Use of the hyphen (-) character in the Host name  field of the Connection  pane of the VHDL Cosimulation Block is not supported.

Summary: Clicking OK does not apply some block property changes
Description: Clicking the OK button on the Block Properties  dialog box does not apply some blocks property changes, e.g., e.g., changes to a block's annotation. This results in loss of the changes.
Workaround: Click the dialog box's Apply button before clicking its OK button.

Summary: Velocity output of the 6DoF ECEF (Quaternion) block
Description: The 6DoF ECEF blocks provide incorrect output for velocity.
Workaround: Installation instructionsThe attached file contains 6DoF ECEF blocks that have been updated to provide the correct output. To use the updated blocks, follow this procedure:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file:/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/aerolib6dof2.mdlTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.

Summary: Non-structure array error when using xPC Target Explorer
Description: If a block label in a Simulink model contains extendedASCII characters (character values between 128 and 255),connecting to a target PC node in xPC Target Explorer produces an error message like the following: Error using> xpcexplrapi>ConnectTGPCNode2Target Attempt to reference field of a non-structured array
Workaround: Quit MATLAB. Rename the following files by appending .old to the filenames: $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\tlc\mdlxml.tlc$MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private\utf8file.dll$MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private\xpcapi.dll$MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\xpcxml2unicode.mDownload the attached zip file and unzip it to the MATLAB root directory. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Triggered subsystems: Trigger block showing output port causes TLC error for models that use asynchronous sample times
Description: If all the following conditions exist for a model, Real-Time Workshop fails to build the generated code due to an unresolved external symbol link error:  The model includes a triggered subsystem. The subsystem is triggered by a periodic signal. The Show output port  option on the Trigger block dialog box is checked. The model uses asynchronous sample times.
Workaround: See the workaround for bug report 249876.

Summary: "Analyzing Multistage Interpolators" tutorial generates an error message
Description: Filter Design Toolbox User's Guide contains two documentation errors that cause two tutorial examples to fail.In the section "Analyzing Multistage Interpolators," two examples include the method dfilt.cascade(h) . When you try to run the examples that include this method, the example returns an error message:??? Error using ==> dfilt.cascade.cascadeCascades must be made of discrete-time (DFILT) objects Here is one of the examples that generates the error.h(1) = mfilt.firinterp(4);h(2) = mfilt.firinterp(2);h(3) = mfilt.firinterp(2);h(4) = mfilt.cicinterp(16);hc = dfilt.cascade(h);
Workaround: Where the method dfilt.cascade appears, replace it with cascade(h). For example, in the following case, cascade(h) replaces dfilt.cascade(h).h(1) = mfilt.firinterp(4);h(2) = mfilt.firinterp(2);h(3) = mfilt.firinterp(2);h(4) = mfilt.cicinterp(16);hc = dfilt.cascade(h);Replace hc = dfilt.cascade(h) with hc = cascade(h).

Summary: Inefficiency slows building of simulation target
Description: An inefficiency in the build process slows down the building of Accelerator simulation targets.

Summary: The La and Lf parameters of the DC Machine block have no effect on the model
Description: The La and Lf parameters of the DC Machine and Discrete DC Machine blocks are not taken into acount by the model.
Workaround: If you are using the previous version, follow these steps to work around this problem:   1. Open the powerlib library. 2. Unlock the powerlib library. 3. Open the Machines sublibrary. 4. Look under the mask of the DC Machine block. 5. Open the 'Ra La' and 'Rf Lf' blocks, then change the Branch Type parameter to 'RL'. 6. Look under the mask of the Discrete DC Machine block. 5. Open the 'Ra La' and 'Rf Lf' blocks, then change the Branch Type parameter to 'RL'. 6. Close the Machines sublibrary, then save powerlib.

Summary: The saturation parameter of the Asynchronous Machine block is not accessible
Description: The saturation parameter of the Asychronous Machine block was introduced in SimPowerSystems 4.1. Unfortunately this new parameter cannot be used because it is disabled in the mask. Enabling this parameter from the powerlib library is not enough to fix the problem: the Simulink model for the machine also needs to be upgraded to take into acount the saturation effect.
Workaround: The saturation parameter can be enabled in SimPowerSystems 4.1 by downloading the following six files, included as attachment.  - Three P coded files that must replace the existing ones: MATLAB704/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/powergui.p MATLAB704/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/private/AsynchronousMachineInit.p MATLAB704/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/private/AsynchronousMachineConvert.p - Two library files: MATLAB704/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/powerlib.mdl MATLAB704/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/powerlib_models.mdl - The updated demo that shows the saturation effect:MATLAB704/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powerdemo/power_asm_sat.mdl The patch has beed verified to work in SPS 4.1 (R14SP2+).

Summary: Lookup Table (n-D) blocks: uncompilable code generated for blocks with 3-D, 4-D, or 5-D tables using 'Evenly Spaced Points' index search algorithm
Description: The Lookup Table (n-D) block generates uncompilable code when you specify Evenly Spaced Points  for the Index search method  parameter with a 3-D, 4-D, or 5-D table.  Blocks configured for 1-D, 2-D, 6-D, or higher dimensioned tables are not affected. This condition is detected by most C compilers as a warning or an error. A typical compiler message is: Error mymodel.c: 17  too many arguments to 'rt_PLookEvnCd'Error mymodel.c: 20  too many arguments to 'rt_PLookEvnCd'Error mymodel.c: 23  too many arguments to 'rt_PLookEvnCd'3 errors, 0 warnings This bug also affects the Simulink Accelerator and the Simulink Model block for simulation.
Workaround: For a 3-D, 4-D, or 5-D table, specify Linear Search or Binary Search for the Lookup Table (n-D) block's Index search method parameter.

Summary: NIDAQ Traditional 7.4 is incompatible with PCI-MIO-16E-1
Description: At higher output rates with version 7.4 of the National Instruments NIDAQ Traditional interface on the National Instruments PCI-MIO-16E-1 board, you might receive the message:??? NI-DAQ: The value of a numeric parameter is invalid. An example of the problem follows:>> ao = analogoutput('nidaq',1);addchannel(ao,0:1);ao.SampleRate = 50000;putdata(ao,ones(ao.SampleRate,2));start(ao)??? NI-DAQ: The value of a numeric parameter is invalid.
Workaround: The release of Version 7.4 of the National Instruments NIDAQ interface software appears to have created a backwards compatibility issue.  Our development team is investigating possible solutions.  Presently, the only available workaround is to revert to NIDAQ Traditional Version 7.3.1.  You must completely uninstall the current National Instruments drivers before installing the earlier release.

Summary: MaxStackSize and MaxStackVariableSize variables: cannot specify at the same time as TLC options
Description: If you specify the MaxStackSize  and MaxStackVariableSize  variables at the same time for the TLC Options  field on the Real-Time Workshop  Configuration Parameters dialog box or the TLCOptions  configuration parameter, you may get an error during the Real-Time Workshop code generation process.
Workaround: Specify MaxStackSize and MaxStackVariableSize in your system target file.

Summary: Measurement Computing PMD-1208FS analog output is 2.4% high
Description: When the following code is executed on the PMD-1208FS device from Measurement Computing, it produces 4.096 volts instead of the expected 4 volts.  All analog outputs are 2.4% above the expected value.ao = analogoutput('mcc',0); %assumes PMD-1208FS is device 0addchannel(ao,0);putsample(ao,4);
Workaround: The output values can be scaled to the correct value by setting the UnitsRange property of the analog output channel(s).  The following code correctly outputs the expected 4.0 volts:ao = analogoutput('mcc',0); %assumes PMD-1208FS is device 0addchannel(ao,0);set(ao.Channel,'UnitsRange',[0 4.096]);putsample(ao,4);

Summary: MaxStackVariableSize variable: incorrect implicit value
Description: If you set the MaxStackVariableSize  variable to inf , Real-Time Workshop implicitly interprets the setting incorrectly as 4096 .

Summary: shapewrite incorrectly writes attribute values -10, -100, -1000, etc. to shapefiles
Description: The shapewrite  function writes an incorrect value when the largest absolute value of an attribute is the negative of a positive power of 10 (e.g., -100), because the undocumented helper function dbffieldinfo  calculates a field length that is too small by one character.  This results in trunction (e.g., -100 becomes -10) when the attribute is written to the DBF file.
Workaround: If you are using Version 2.1 of the Mapping Toolbox, you can apply the following workaround:In your MATLAB root directory, locate the file toolbox/map/mapformats/dbffieldinfo.m and change line 38 from:                numLeftOfDecimal = ceil(log10(max(abs(v))));to:                numLeftOfDecimal = max(1, 1 + floor(log10(max(abs(v)))));or replace this file with the version in the attachment.

Summary: Code generation failure in models with both SimMechanics and SimDriveline blocks
Description: Code generation from a model that contains both SimMechanics and SimDriveline blocks fails at the linking stage because of multiply-defined symbols. The pre-compiled code libraries that ship with each product contain utility functions with the same name.

Summary: Action subsystems: If Action Subsystem or Switch Case Action Subsystem block in asynchronous subsystem cannot be reused from different sample times
Description: During code generation for a model, Real-Time Workshop omits code for If Action Subsystem or Switch Case Action Subsystem blocks in asynchronous subsystems if all the following conditions exist: The model includes multiple instances of an If Action Subystem or Switch Case Action Subsystem block. The subsystem is reusable. That is, the RTW system code  option on the subsystem block parameter dialog box is set to Reusable function  or Auto . At least one instance of the subsystem is in an asynchronous function call hierarchy while another instance is in a periodic task.
Workaround: If you are using R14SP2, replace matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\if.tlc and matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\case.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\if.tlc tomatlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\if.tlc.old.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\case.tlc tomatlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\case.tlc.old.Download the attached files and place them in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocksRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Accelerated Simulation produces incorrect results with Signal Storage Reuse on
Description: The Simulink Accelerator can produce wrong answers due to incorrectly generated code in which a local variable is overwritten before the variable's final use.
Workaround: Turning off the "Signal Storage Reuse" optimization avoids this bug.

Summary: Using eigs on a 64-bit Linux platform with an Intel EM64t processor generates undefined symbol error.
Description: Using eigs  on a 64-bit Linux platform with a Intel EM64t processor generates the following error: >> a = eigs(eye(3))??? Invalid MEX file '/opt/matlab7/toolbox/matlab/sparfun/arpackc.mexa64': /opt/matlab7/bin/glnxa64/libmwarpack.so: undefined symbol: s_cmp.

Summary: gauges_register_ocx fails if MATLAB installation directory contains space
Description: If the name of the MATLAB installation directory contains a space character, then the gauges_register_ocx  function produces an error message instead of registering the ActiveX controls.
Workaround: To work around this problem:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file by appending .old to the filename:   $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges\gauges_register_ocx.p$MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by entering   matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.Download the attached file and place it in the directory mentioned in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:   rehash toolboxcache   gauges_register_ocx

Summary: Math block: inefficient code generated for block when configured to execute the mod function
Description: If you configure the Math block to execute the mod  function and connect a Constant block to the second input port, Real-Time Workshop might generate inefficient code.

Summary: The PHASEZ function errors out when inputs are column vectors
Description: The phasez  function errors when the b  and a  inputs that represent the numerator and denominator coefficients are column vectors.
Workaround: Change the b and a coefficients into row vectors.

Summary: Reaction torque measurement in 2D mode
Description: When you simulate a 2D mechanism whose Machine Environment block has Machine dimensionality  set to 2D Only  or Auto-detect , SimMechanics can incorrectly report the reaction torques on Joints, Constraints, or Drivers.
Workaround: If you are measuring reaction torques on Joints, Constraints, or Drivers, make sure the Machine Environment block has Machine dimensionality set to 3D Only.If your model does not measure reaction torques on Joints, Constraints, or Drivers, no changes are necessary.

Summary: Section word lengths for CIC Decimators can grow larger than Bmax
Description: When a CIC Decimator filter (mfilt.cicdecim ) has its SectionWordLengthMode  set to MinWordLengths  and you increase the OutputWordLength  to larger than the input word length, the word lengths of the individual sections can grow to beyond Bmax . For more information about Bmax , the most significant bit of CIC filters, enter help mfilt/filtmsb  at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Accumulator pattern may generate incorrect simulation result
Description: If your model contains an accumulator pattern and the Sum block in the pattern specifies different data types for its input and ouput ports, simulating the model may produce incorrect results (see "Accumulators" in "Performance Tuning" in "Optimizing the Model for Code Generation" in the Real-Time Workshop documentation for more information).
Workaround: The following are workarounds for this problem:Set the Sum block in all accumulator patterns in your block diagram to Require all inputs to have same data type.Turn off the block reduction optimization in the block diagram.

Summary: Segmentation violation when modeling small inertias
Description: In certain instances, models with small inertias cause SimDriveline to crash.  "Small" in this context means considerably smaller than the default inertia value for the Inertia block.

Summary: Asynchronous subsystems: incorrect code generated for cascaded function calls in an asynchronous function call hierarchy
Description: Real-Time Workshop duplicates the code it generates for a cascaded function call subsystem under the following conditions: The asynchronous function call subsystem includes a function call generator. The function call generator calls cascaded function call subsystems that reside outside the boundaries of the asynchronous subsystem.
Workaround: If you are using R14SP2 or R14SP2+, replace  matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc to matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc.old.Download commonbodlib.tlc.R14sp2dacore, rename the file to commonbodlib.tlc, and place it in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Stage input fraction length is disabled when Avoid overflow is cleared
Description: In the quantization panel of FDATool, the Stage input fraction length  option is disabled even when the Avoid overflow  checkbox is cleared.  This is a drawing error.  The Avoid overflow  checkbox should be selected by default, which disables the edit box.
Workaround: Select and clear the Avoid overflow checkbox to enable the associated Stage input fraction length option.

Summary: Spectrogram errors when ploting PSD estimate using frequency vector
Description: An error occurs if you use spectrogram  with no output arguments and specify a vector of frequencies to compute the spectrogram.

Summary: CANdb Packing / Unpacking blocks fail to read CANdb DBC files that contain large numerical values
Description: When using the CANdb Packing / Unpacking blocks, CANdb DBC files that contain CANdb Signal properties, minimum, maximum, offset or factor, with values that are outside the range of a signed 32-bit integer value do not parse correctly. An example of an out of range value is 4211081215.

Summary: Generated MATLAB Code for imported SOS filter in FDATool is wrong
Description: When you import a second-order section (SOS) filter into FDATool, and then generate MATLAB code for the filter, the generated MATLAB Code does not create an SOS filter.

Summary: Model reference: builds fail when a signal is a test point and has storage class other than auto
Description: When you build a model that meets the following criteria, the build can fail with a linking error that indicates an undefined reference to the signal identifier:  Contains a referenced model   The referenced model has a signal at its boundary that is a test point and has a storage class other than auto  The failure occurs because the identifier for the signal is missing from the generated code.

Summary: Multirate model with algebraic loop causes a segV
Description: Simulating a model that contains an algebraic loop involving a single, multirate block causes a memory segmentation violation (segV) error.

Summary: The Sequence Viewer is unable to import workspace sequence with length between 1024 and 1300.
Description: If you select Import from Workspace  menu item in the Sequen Viewer to import a sequence from the MATLAB workspace, and the sequence length happen to be between 1024 and 1300. An error message dialog will popup and warn that exist(fullfile,...) is failing with the long sequence .

Summary: Reusable subsystems: when used with displayable output generated code functions incorrectly
Description: A model's simulation behavior can differ from the behavior of the generated code if the model contains a subsystem that meets the following criteria   Is configured to generate a reusable function  Has an output signal that is displayable (test point or storage class other than auto)  Is conditionally executed (for example, an enabled subsystem) with an empty initial value (for example, instead of an explicit value such as 0)
Workaround: Place a Signal Conversion block at the output of the subsystem for which you intend to generate a reusable function Move any signal label at that output to the output of the Signal Conversion block. Configure the Signal Conversion block as follows:  Set the Output parameter to Contiguous copy Select the Override optimizations and always copy signal option

Summary: Generated code includes real_T terms with Support floating point numbers deselected
Description: Stateflow charts that contain fixed-point data with no scaling and binary points (for example, equivalent pure integers) cause extraneous real_T  casts in the generated code.
Workaround: Assign the types of this data to integer instead of fixed-point with no scaling.

Summary: Derivative or zero-crossing functions: calls to subsystem output code for GRT and GRT malloc targets result in generated code that fails to compile
Description: If the folowing conditions exist for a model, generated code fails to compile due to an undeclared task identifier (tid ): You specify the GRT and GRT malloc target. A derivative or zero-crossing function calls subsystem output code.
Workaround: Replace the file matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\subsystm.tlc  as follows:  Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt. Quit MATLAB. Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\subsystm.tlc to matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks\subsystm.tlc.old. Download the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\blocks Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Test benches for cascades of single-rate and interpolating filters do not drive the inputs properly
Description: Generated test benches for cascades of single-rate and interpolating filters do not drive the data inputs properly, causing the first data input value to be missed. Since multi-rate filters are not linear time invariant, missing this one data input causes the entire filter output to produce incorrect values.

Summary: Discontinuous signal at asynchronous subsystem port causes incorrect results
Description: If a signal at an asynchronous function-call subsystem output port is not contiguous, the rate transition block connected to this port does not work correctly.

Summary: Async Interrupt blocks: compile error can occur for models that include an Async Interrupt block and triggered subsystem
Description: If the following conditions exist for a model, the model can fail to compile: Includes a triggered subsystem that is configured for the Real-Time Workshop function or reusable function code format The Show output port  option is enabled for that subsystem's trigger port The model includes an Async Interrupt block   Compilation fails due to the generation of undefined symbols of the format subsystem_name TIDnum  , where subsystem_name   is the name of the triggered subsystem with the trigger port set to show its output port and num   is a numeric task identifier.
Workaround: Replace the TLC file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/blocks/subsystm.tlc in your MATLAB installation area with the attached version of subsystm.tlc.  Determine the root directory of your MATLAB installation by entering the matlabroot command in the MATLAB Command Window.Quit MATLAB. Rename matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/blocks/subsystm.tlc to subsystm.tlc.old.Download the attached version of subsystm.tlc to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/blocks.Restart MATLAB. Enter the MATLAB command rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: xPC Target commands do not work with the MATLAB Compiler after running stand-alone applications
Description: You can use the MATLAB Compiler to create a stand-alone application from a MATLAB file function calling xPC Target commands. However, running the stand-alone application produces the following error: Undefined function or variable 'xpcroot'.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file by appending .old to the filename: $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\xpcroot.mDownload the attached zip file and unzip it to the MATLAB root directory.(where you can find the MATLAB root directory by entering  matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.)Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error when modifying the nominal parameters of the psbhysteresis tool of the Powergui block
Description: There is a bug in the psbhysteresis interface of the Powergui block, when you try to modify the value of the nominal parameters. When you change one of the nominal parameters, you get the following error: Function 'eval' is not defined for values of class 'double'.

Summary: Video Input block's Cancel button operates as an OK button.
Description: If changes are made to the Video Input block mask, and then the Cancel button is pressed, the changes made to the mask are applied and the mask is closed. There is no way to cancel and undo the changes made to the Video Input block mask.

Summary: Moving unconjugated poles/zeros in SPTool pole/zero editor causes errors
Description: If you have FVTool open and you attempt to move unconjugated poles or zeros in the SPTool Pole/Zero editor, errors are reported in the command window.

Summary: Video Input block does not allow selection of devices having the same name
Description: If multiple devices with the same name are connected, the Video Input block only displays a single name that is permanently associated with first device. The block does not allow you to select each device separately.

Summary: HIST function plots have wider end bins than previous versions did
Description: The hist  command now extends the X range of the bins on the ends of the plot to include the entire data range in the case when the user-specified range does not cover the data range. For example,data = [ 1 3 9 4 5 7 1 3 9 40 3 3]; hist(data,1:5)  will now extend the rightmost bin to include x=40 .
Workaround: To create a histogram-like plot with equally-sized bins use the bar command:data = [ 1 3 9 4 5 7 1 3 9 40 3 3];[n,x]=hist(data,1:5);bar(x,n,1)

Summary: GetSet CSC: generated code does not include specified SetFunction
Description: If you use the GetSet  custom storage class (CSC) to generate specialized function calls, for example, to access the memory associated with a Data Store Memory block, Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder might not use the SetFunction  property, even if specified.
Workaround: Before generating code, disable expression folding by clearing the Eliminate superfluous temporary variables (Expression folding) option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Why does the videoinput function error for certain DCAM hardware
Description: When I try to connect to my hardware using the DCAM adaptor with the videoinput  function I receive the error: ??? Attempt to divide an integer value by an integer divisor of zero.  This error is received even though the camera is recognized by imaqhwinfo .
Workaround: This bug only affects certain formats.  Try calling the videoinput function with a different format.  Formats are specified as the third input argument to the videoinput function.

Summary: Model reference disregards case differences in model names
Description: The Model block may not distinguish between models whose names differ only in case. This can lead to unpredictable simulation results if the MATLAB path contains models whose names differ only in case.
Workaround: To work around this problem, ensure that: The specified Model name on the Model block is an exact (case-sensitive) match with the referenced model name.There are no open models or any models on the MATLAB path whose names match regardless of case.

Summary: Reusable functions: Real-Time Workshop fails to reuse identical Stateflow charts
Description: Real-Time Workshop fails to create one reusable function for identical Stateflow charts, even though the System Code  option of the chart is set to Reusable Function .
Workaround: Make sure that every identical Stateflow chart has exactly the same name.

Summary: Configuration Parameters output logging is disabled in xPC Target Explorer
Description: Configuration Parameters output logging is disabled in xPC Target Explorer. The output logging selection (Logging ) is always grayed out. This happens even if the model contains outputs and you have enabled output logging through the Simulation>Configuration Parameters  dialog box.
Workaround: As a workaround,Stop MATLAB.Rename the following files to updatexpcexplr.m.old and xpcmngrPanelEvent.m.old.  matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\xpcmngr\updatexpcexplr.mmatlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\xpcmngr\xpcmngrPanelEvent.mDownload the attached files and place them in the directory mentioned in Step 2.Restart MATLAB.In the MATLAB Command Window, type: rehash toolbox Rebuild your model.

Summary: CAN frame type of the TouCAN Receive block CAN message output is always "standard"
Description: In the real-time application, the frame type of the TouCAN Receive block CAN message output is always "standard".   This is the case, even if the TouCAN Receive block is configured to receive "extended" messages, and the Simulink Port Data Type is displayed as "CAN_MESSAGE_EXTENDED".This can be a problem if the CAN message output of the block is directly connected to a TouCAN Transmit block.   In this case the CAN message frame type that is transmitted will always be "standard".
Workaround: Please do not directly connect the CAN message output of the TouCAN Receive block to a TouCAN Transmit block.Instead, you can use the CAN Message Unpacking block to unpack the data and then use the CAN Message Packing block to repack the data into a CAN message of the appropriate frame type.

Summary: To File block eliminated during code generation for S-Function target
Description: When generating code for an S-Function target, Real-Time Workshop might remove a To File block from a model or subsystem to optimize the generated code.  If this optimization occurs, the code does not write to the specified file when executing and no MAT file is created.
Workaround: When generating code for an S-Function, clear the Block reduction optimization option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Performance improvement instructions missing from documentation
Description: Some useful tips on how to achieve performance enhancements are missing from the documentation.
Workaround: The following tips can significantly enhance performance. Run the Model Advisor.Following the suggestions in the Model Advisor report may result in faster on target execution. Increase the System Clock beyond the default 20 MHz.The default System Clock Frequency is 20MHz.  For higher performance you should consider increasing the System Clock Frequency up to 40MHz, which is the maximum for the MPC555 device. Other processor variants may support higher System Clock Frequencies depending on your development board.   Please consult your development board documentation for details.For more information see these User's Guide sections in the documentation:The MPC555 Resource Configuration block for information on how to change system clock parameters. The Switch Target Configuration block - this is a utility block you can use to apply some pre-defined configurations. Configure the model to run from internal Flash (rather than external RAM) because this has faster memory access.     Altering the fetch serialization mode can offer more than double the performance.See solution 1-14ZN0X "Can I get performance improvement on FetchSerialization (ICTRL) in  the Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 Version 2.0.2 (R14SP2)?" In release R14SP2 and previous releases of Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555, the MPC555 is configured to run in "serialize mode". This reduces the performance of the processor in comparison with the alternative non-serialized mode of operation. When using the TouCAN Interrupt Generator block you can improve performance as follows:Disable "Use floating point" in the TouCAN Interrupt Generator (if possible). This will save significant time during ISR context switches (of which there may be many, depending on application).Minimise the code that runs in the context of the ISR. Try and move as much code out of the ISR (function-call subsystem) as possible to speed up individual ISR's.   This should allow an increase in the rate at which CAN messages can be received on that buffer.

Summary: Cannot set UnitsRange or SensorRange to values with negative slope
Description: If you attempt to set UnitRange  or SensorRange  to a value whose first element is greater than the second element, such as [10 0] , you get the message:??? Error using ==> daqchild.setProperty must be a 1x2 vector of scalar values with first value less than second value. This prevents the scaling of sampled data using a negative slope.
Workaround: To produce a negative slope, you must scale your raw data using MATLAB.  The MATLAB function interp1 performs 1-D interpolation and table lookup.  (Note that the function name interp1 ends in the number one (1), not the letter 'L'.)

Summary: ping Function Issues Warning About Structure Assignment to Non-Empty
Description: When using the ping  function, the following warning message appears: Warning: Because of a bug, MATLAB has allowed structure assignment to a non-empty non-structure to overwrite the previous value. This behavior will continue in this release, but will be an error in a future version of MATLAB. For advice on how to write code that will both avoid this warning and work in future versions of MATLAB, see R14SP2 Release Notes. In database.ping at 57
Workaround: While this is a bug, the warning message is benign and there should be no impact on the operation of ping. You can suppress the warning message using the following code:warning off MATLAB:warn_r14_stucture_assignment

Summary: Fixed step size mismatch for model reference is incorrectly allowed
Description: When a referenced model uses a fixed-step solver and has both a continuous and discrete task, the fixed step size of the referenced model must match the fixed step size of the top model. This error is incorrectly controlled by the Automatic solver parameter selection  diagnostic.  So setting the diagnostic to None  or Warning  allows you to simulate the model using this incorrect configuration, which may produce incorrect answers.

Summary: Rate Transition block: incorrectly inserted between source block and Merge block
Description: If you select the Automatically handle rate transfer  option on the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, Real-Time Workshop inserts a hidden Rate Transition block between a Merge block and its source block if the two blocks have different sample times.  Any block inserted between a source block and a Merge block must preserve the execution context of the source. A Rate Transition block does not meet this requirement and should not be inserted in this context.
Workaround: Clear the checkbox Automatically handle rate transfer on the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Option FunValCheck produces an error in fminsearch
Description: fminsearch  displays the following error if the option FunValCheck is set to 'on' and the objective function returns an invalid value (NaN or complex):??? Error using ==> errorFormatted arguments cannot be non-scalar numeric matrices.

Summary: Editor/Debugger: Incorrect Selection Sometimes Pasted to Other Application
Description: If you copy a selection from the Editor/Debugger and paste it to another application, the pasted selection might be incorrect when the Editors/Debugger preference for Tab key inserts spaces  is not selected.
Workaround: In Preferences > Editor/Debugger > Keyboard & Indenting, select the option Tab key inserts spaces.

Summary: Reusable function call subsystems:  code generation error occurs with feedback loop and displayable output
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates an error if all the following conditions apply: The RTW system code  option on the Block Parameters dialog box for a function call subsystem is set to Reusable function . An output of that function call subsystem is involved in a feedback loop to the subsystem. The output signal has global storage, such as a test point.

Summary: Reusable subsystem with displayable output to Merge block
Description: A error can occur during code generation if all of the following conditions pertain to a model: The model contains a subsystem for which the RTW system code  option on the Block Parameters dialog box is set to Reusable function  Output of that subsystem is displayable (for example, a test point) That output provides input for a Merge block
Workaround: For Release 14 SP2, set the RTW system code option on the Block Parameters dialog box to Auto.

Summary: Gamma Correction block might produce incorrect results
Description: The Gamma Correction block produces incorrect results if, for the Operation parameter, you select De-gamma and you select the Linear segment check box.

Summary: PDF Files Do Not Open from Help Browser on Windows Platforms
Description: On Windows platforms, when you click a link in the Help browser to open a printable version of a product's documentation (PDF file), the PDF file does not open and an error like the following appears: There is no application associated with the given file name extension. The Help browser cannot locate Adobe Acrobat to open the file.
Workaround: One workaround is to access the PDF documentation from the Documentation area of the MathWorks Web site.Another workaround is to edit the MATLAB openpdf.m file by performing the following steps. Note that in general, modifying files in the matlabroot\toolbox directories is not recommended.Identify the location of Adobe Acrobat on your PC, for example, D:\Applications\acrobat 5.0\Reader\AcroRd32.exeAs a precaution, make a copy of the file matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\general\private\openpdf.m (where matlabroot is the directory returned when you run thematlabroot function) in the same directory and rename it to openpdf.old.Open the openpdf.m file in the Editor/Debugger or another text editor.Replace line 12 in openpdf.m, which uses the winopen function, with the following statement:system(['"Adobe_Acrobat_Path" ' fileName ' &']);where Adobe_Acrobat_Path is the location of Acrobat Reader on your PC as determined in the first step.

Summary: Section and Output fraction lengths for CIC decimators are incorrect
Description: The SectionFracLengths  and OutputFracLength  property values computed by mfilt.cicdecim  filter object are not correct.  The calculation does not account for the dynamic range of the input.

Summary: Code reuse: multiple instances of reusable subsystem that have displayable signals downstream can fail to reuse function
Description: Multiple instances of a reusable subsystem that have displayable signals downstream can fail to reuse a function due to unintended consequences of the following: Block reduction Signal label propagation from a higher level of the subsystem hierarchy
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block at the output port of the subsystem for which you intend to generate a reusable function. Move any signal label at the subsystem's output port to the output port of the Signal Conversion block. Configure the Signal Conversion block as follows:Set Output to Contiguous copy.Select Override optimizations and always copy signal

Summary: From/To xPC Target blocks do not work in subsystem
Description: If you use the From xPC Target or To xPC Target blocks in a subsystem,xPC Target returns the error SubSystem block does not have a parameter named SystemTargetFile . The model will no longer work.
Workaround: Download the following zip file and unzip it to the MATLAB root directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Preview window errors with invalid width and height ROIPosition values
Description: After configuring a video input object's ROIPosition  property to a width and height of 200, the Preview window errors, indicating that the width and height values must be positive. vid = videoinput('dcam', 1);             set(vid,'ROIPosition',[0 0 200 200])     preview(vid)                             ??? Error using ==> set                  Width and height must be > 0.             Other ROIPosition property values may also encounter this error.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, adjust the ROIPosition values by one pixel. This error is only encountered for a few ROIPosition values.

Summary: funcCount field in output structure is incorrect in fgoalattain, fmincon, fminimax when both objective and constraint gradients are supplied
Description: The number of evaluations for user-supplied functions was incorrect in the functions fgoalattain , fmincon , and fminimax , in the specific case that the gradients of the objective and constraint functions were supplied and the options GradObj  and GradConstr  were both set to 'on' .

Summary: Blocks incorrectly throwing errors when placed in periodic subsystem
Description: Certain blocks incorrectly cause an error when they are placed in a periodic (atomic or function-call) subsystem. The problematic blocks are blocks with specified port-based sample times where all of the ports have the sample time specified for the periodic subsystem. An example of this type of block is the Model block.

Summary: impixelregionpanel errors when the parent is a uipanel object
Description: The impixelregionpanel  function fails when passed a handle to a uipanel object for its hparent  argument. The function outputs the following error message: ??? Error using ==> set Invalid default property for uipanel property: 'DefaultUipanelHitTest'.

Summary: Polynomial model description may display incorrect sign of coefficient
Description: For certain polynomial models that have been created in the Model Browser, the Model Description window will display the incorrect sign for the first term that is in the model, for example "2*X1 + 3*X2" instead of "-2*X1 + 3*X2".  The underlying model that is used within the Model Browser is correct and the model is evaluated correctly at all times.  However if the coefficients are copied to use in an evaluation elsewhere then incorrect values will be produced.This issue only occurs for polynomial models that have no constant term and have a negative coefficient value for the first term that is in the model.
Workaround: This problem can be fixed by updating the function that converts the model into a string for display:     Close MATLAB    Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcmodels\@xregcubic\char.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcmodels\@xregcubic\char_old.m    Download the file char.m and place it in the directory $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcmodels\@xregcubic    Restart MATLAB

Summary: Tasks and jobs can get stuck in running state after job manager crash and recovery
Description: When a job manager crashes, all its jobs in the 'running'  state keep their State  property set to 'running'  unless they have a timeout set. Tasks without timeouts that were running at the time of the crash keep their State  property value of 'running' when the job manager comes back up, but these tasks do not complete after the job manager recovers. Therefore, tasks that are running without timeouts at the time the job manager crashes, remain stuck in the 'running'  state after the job manager recovers.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, always set the Timeout property on all jobs and tasks for some reasonable time limit.

Summary: hgload with structure input allows invalid property values to be set
Description: The hgload  command lets users instantiate a structure and specify its default properties. If this structure contains data for properties that are read only - specifically the ApplicationData  property - the handle created by calling hgload  can have invalid data for such properties as a result.
Workaround: Avoid using ApplicationData in the property structure passed to hgload.

Summary: loadlibrary fails when MATLAB is installed in a directory having a space in its name
Description: If MATLAB is installed in a directory that has a space in its name, then loading a library produces an error like:??? Error using ==> loadlibrary Failed to preprocess the input file. Output from preprocessor is:'C:\Program' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file.
Workaround: In R14sp2 modify line 168 of loadlibrary.m in toolbox\matlab\general from:cc=[lcc ' -noregistrylookup' ccinclude ' -E "' header '"'];to:cc=['"' lcc '" -noregistrylookup' ccinclude ' -E "' header '"'];If your header has #include  statements, then it might also be neccesarey to modify the following lines:line 106 from: ccinclude = [ccinclude ' -I ' fullfile(matlabroot, 'extern','include')]; to: ccinclude = [ccinclude ' -I "' fullfile(matlabroot,'extern','?include"')]; line 166 from: ccinclude = [ccinclude ' -I ' fullfile(matlabroot,'sys',...'lcc','include')]; to: ccinclude = [ccinclude ' -I "' fullfile(matlabroot,'sys',...'lcc','include"')]; The line numbers might be different in previous releases, but the changes are the same.

Summary: From Wave Device and To Wave Device blocks cause errors with RTW C++ code generation
Description: Models containing From Wave Device or To Wave Device blocks from the Signal Processing Blockset error out when you generate C++ code using Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: Datatips return incorrect interpolated result
Description: The following code produces a Nichols plot:G = tf(200,conv([1 10],[1 4 10])) figure nichols(G,'k') grid  After executing this code, click on the data cursor button, then click on the solid line close to the root locus to bring up the data cursor window. Click at about -10 dB and drag the data cursor to the right.  You will notice that the frequency readings increase from -7 to -6, and then decrease to -7 again. This behavior happens in other intervals as well.

Summary: FDATool Chebyshev II filter with normalized frequency errors
Description: Designing a Chebyshev II filter with normalized frequency in FDATool produces an error.

Summary: urlread cannot read multibyte characters
Description: urlread  incorrectly converts all multibyte characters to zeros in the returned string.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the MATLAB file. Here are the steps:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following MATLAB file: $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/iofun/urlread.m(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.) Rename the file to urlread.m.old.Download the attached file, urlread.m. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: CANdb Packing / Unpacking blocks fail to load CANdb .dbc files containing "Value Tables"
Description: CANdb Packing / Unpacking blocks fail to load CANdb .dbc files containing "Value Tables".
Workaround: Please do not try to load CANdb .dbc files that contain "Value Tables".

Summary: Data types of some objects can be incorrectly determined
Description: Simulink may incorrectly determine the data type specified (as a string) by the DataType  property of Simulink.Signal  objects and the BaseType  attribute of Simulink.AliasType  objects. This can cause Simulink to assign incorrect or invalid data types to some such objects during model compilation.

Summary: Rate Conversion block adds extra zeros to the output
Description: The Signal Processing Blockset Rate Conversion block adds unexpected leading zeros to the output in some cases. These zeros are not present in the result you receive if you perform the separate steps of upsampling, filtering, and downsampling. In particular, the current behavior does not match the upfirdn  function in the Signal Processing Toolbox nor does it match the MFILT.FIRSRC multirate filter in the Filter Design Toolbox.

Summary: Stateflow generates incorrect code when passing an array to a legacy function
Description: From a Stateflow chart, calls to custom-code functions that involve vector data generate incorrect code for a Real-Time Workshop build. For example, a call like the following cmp1 = strcmp(str1,str2) passes str1  and str2  correctly for a simulation build, but incorrectly for a code generation build.
Workaround: As a workaround, use an explicit address operator, for example:strcmp(&str1[0],&str2[0])

Summary: Failure to print to certain printers on Mac OS X 10.3
Description: When you send a figure to a printer on Mac OS X 10.3, the figure's print dialog shows a list of printer names (which may be diferent from the print queue names). Printing to any printer having a device name that differs from the name of its queue causes MATLAB to give an error and fail to print, as follows:??? Error using ==> printdlg (LocalJavaPrintDlg) Error using ==> print

Summary: If you open multiple imcontrast tools for a particular image, the additional tools error when you close them.
Description: If you open more than one imconstrast  tool for an image displayed with imshow , the additional imcontrast  tools error when you close them.

Summary: medfilt2 segmentation violation with uint8 data when neighborhood is larger than 10-by-10
Description: The medfilt2  function fails with a segmentation violation when used with uint8  data when the neighborhood is 10-by-10 or larger.

Summary: National Instruments PCI-6013 and PCI-6014 boards do not support InputType 'SingleEnded'
Description: When attempting a single-ended acquisition using a National Instruments (NI) PCI-6013 or PCI-6014 acquisition card, by executing  ai = analoginput('nidaq',2);  % adjust to the ID of your CI-6014  ai.InputType = 'SingleEnded';  addchannel(ai,daqhwinfo(ai,'SingleEndedIDs'));  start(ai) you get the error "??? NI-DAQ: The value of a numeric parameter is invalid."
Workaround: The National Instruments (NI) PCI-6013 or PCI-6014 acquisition cards do not support a referenced single-ended acquisition.  Only differential and nonreferenced single-ended acquisitions are supported.  Although the toolbox offers the option of 'SingleEnded' for the InputType property, you must choose 'NonReferencedSingleEnded' or 'Differential'.  Consult your board's documentation for the correct method for wiring signals to your board in these modes.

Summary: Obscure error in linprog large scale
Description: In some cases (when the "lambda" output is required,there are only inequality constraints, the "b" input is all zeros,the elements in input A satisfy certain scaling condition),the large scale method in linprog  returns the following error message:  ??? Undefined function or variable "q".  Error in ==> optim\private\lipsol at 1103            y = y .* rowscl * q; This is due to an internal variable not being initialized.

Summary: Bus Selector performs some tasks slowly on deep bus hierarchies
Description: The Bus Selector takes a while to perform actions on large, highly hierarchical buses, such as selecting or removing a signal.

Summary: W2 edit box in ccsfirdemo gets disabled when you change W1 edit box
Description: When you change the value in the W1 text box in the ccsfirdemo , the W2 text box stops working. You cannot change the entry. To demonstrate this error, do the following steps. Run ccsfirdemo.  Change the value in the W1 edit box and hit Enter.  Notice that the W2 edit box gets disabled and therefore cannot be modified.The W2 field is disabled because it is not relevant when the filter design chosen is 'Low Pass' or 'High Pass'.

Summary: Function call output may not be correct in CCS 3.0
Description: When you perform a function call, the output may not be correct if you use the following software configuration:  CCS version: 3.0Link for Code Composer Studio version: 1.4 or 1.4.1 When yiou use the above configuration, CCS 3.0 rearranges the DSP function declaration inputs into alphabetical order. For example, when you run a function that has the following prototype: void foo(int z, int x, int y) Link for CCS Development Tools interprets this as void foo(int x, int y, int z) Notice the change in the order of the input parameters. As a result, the value you intend to pass to input z is set instead to input x, and so on. This bug results from using CCS 3.0 which sorts the function input parameters in alphabetical order.
Workaround: To avoid this unintended assignment of the input values, use one of the following workarounds.Workaround 1:Pass the function declaration directly to createobj during construction.For example:ff = createobj(cc,'foo','function','funcdecl','void foo(int z, int x, int y)')Workaround 2: Install a patchDownload and open attachment_360879_20070228.zip. Extract the file, LfCpatch258843.exe, to the MATLAB root folder. (e.g., C:\Program Files\MATLAB\)Navigate to MATLAB root folder.Double-click LfCpatch258843.exe. This is a self-extracting archive. A dialog opens.For "Unzip to folder" enter a period (.), and then click Unzip.A dialog saying "2 file(s) unzipped successfully" opens. Click OK to close the dialog.Open MATLAB.At the command prompt, type rehash toolboxreset

Summary: ET for HC12 models do not compile with CodeWarrior 3.1
Description: When compiling an ET for a HC12 model with CodeWarrior 3.1, you will get compilation errors.

Summary: Using vector data in transition-guarding conditions causes assertion failures
Description: Using vector data in transition-guarding conditions causes assertion failures during code generation.
Workaround: This is an unsupported operation. Do not use a vector as a condition for a transition.

Summary: Build failure when MATLABROOT contains spaces and "Use prebuilt RTW libraries" is unchecked
Description: A build error similar to that below occurs if the path to your MATLAB installation (MATLABROOT) contains spaces AND the "Use prebuilt RTW libraries" Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 RTW option is unchecked.Note that this is actually a Real-Time Workshop build process bug rather than an Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 bug.Build error:NMAKE : fatal error U1073: don't know how to make 'rt_atan2.obj' Stop.
Workaround: Please install MATLAB in a path that does not contain spaces.

Summary: Linearization of multirate models can be inaccurate under certain conditions
Description: The linearization of multirate models with more then one discrete sample rate can sometimes result in an inaccurate linearization result when there is only one state at each sample rate.
Workaround: To fix this problem, save the attached file jacobian2ss.m to the $matlabroot\toolbox\slcontrol\slctrlutil\ directory on your system. Before saving the attached file, you might want to create a backup of the old jacobian2ss.m file by renaming it jacobian2ss_old.m.

Summary: System target files: changes that cause recursive actions can produce a segmentation violation
Description: If you modify or create a system target file (STF) such that it includes target callbacks that cause recursive actions, Simulink may generate a segmentation violation when you select the target. Examples of changes that produce such results include target callbacks that use switchTarget  or set_param(model, 'RTWOptions', ...).
Workaround: Modify the system target file to conform to the system target file structure documented in the "System Target Files" section of the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Code Developing Embedded Targerts documentation. In particular, avoid using functions that can cause recursive actions, such as switchTarget or set_param(model, 'RTWOptions', ...), in target callbacks.

Summary: Right-clicking Simulink blocks can cause a SegV when Simulink Verification and Validation is installed
Description: Right-clicking Simulink blocks in the Model Editor may cause a memory segmentation violation when Simulink Verification and Validation is installed. In this case, Simulink generates a stack trace that begins as follows: Stack Trace:  [0] simulink.dll:bool __cdecl isInputPortsEnabled(struct inRec_tag*,void *)(0x0ebad3c0, 0, 0x7a10e7d0, 0x7a10a2f0) + 23 bytes  [1] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl mi_CreateAllDynamicComponents(struct WinRec_tag *,struct slMenuInfo_tag *,bool)(0x0ebad3c0, 0, 1, 0x01cc2da0) + 96 bytes  [2] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl mi_CreateAllDynamicComponents(struct WinRec_tag *,struct slMenuInfo_tag *,bool)(0x0ebad3c0, 36, 1, 0x7a109f88) + 158 bytes  [3] simulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl create_context_menu(struct WinRec_tag *,enum slContextMenuType_tag,struct slMenuInfo_tag * *)(0x00cdae44, 0x0eb449a0, 0x01cc2da0, 0x012e8328) + 107 bytes  [4] simulink.dll:void __cdecl manage_context_menu(struct slGraph_tag *,struct mwpoint const *,class SlEvent *)(0x01cc2da0, 0x00cdaef4, 0x012e00de, 0x0eb449a0) + 208 bytes  [5] simulink.dll:void __cdecl DefaultBlockHandleClickFcn(struct slGraph_tag *,struct slBlock_tag *,class SlEvent *,struct mwpoint *)(0x01cc2da0, 0x0fc20ce0, 3048232, 0x00cdaef4) + 397 bytes  [6] simulink.dll:void __cdecl BlockHandleClick(struct slGraph_tag *,struct slBlock_tag *,void *,struct mwpoint *)(0x01cc2da0, 0x0eb449a0, 0x012e8328, 0x00cdaef4) + 25 bytes  [7] simulink.dll:void __cdecl bdeButtonDownFcn(struct WinRec_tag *,class SlEvent *)(0x0ebad3c0, 0x012e8328, 0x0ebad3c0, 0x00cdaf24 "  [8] simulink.dll:public: static void __cdecl SlUserEvent::sendUserEvent(void (__cdecl*)(struct WinRec_tag *,class SlEvent *),struct WinRec_tag *,void *)(0x79b37bf0, 0x0ebad3c0, 0x00cdaf5c, 0x00cdaf3c " ??") + 197 bytes  [9] simulink.dll:_bdeButtonDownWrapper(0x0ebad3c0, 0x00cdaf5c, 0x0ebad3c0, 0x1005a270) + 21 bytes  [10] libmwgui.dll:void __cdecl wm_ButtondownFcn(struct WinRec_tag *,void *)(0x0ebad3c0, 0x00cdaf5c, 1, 0x1005a270) + 24 bytes

Summary: Type II highpass filters cannot be designed with window method in FDATool
Description: Type II highpass filters cannot be designed with the window method in FDATool.

Summary: Find and Replace hangs or fails to work in the Embedded MATLAB Editor
Description: If you use the Find and Replace  dialog of the Embedded MATLAB Editor to find a non-existent string, MATLAB might hang or emit Java exceptions.

Summary: Model with inconsistent Hardware implementation settings can be simulated
Description: Simulink can incorrectly allow you to simulate a model whose Hardware Implementation  settings conflict with those of models that it references. This can occur if the first words of the incompatible settings are the same, for example, 32bit Generic  and 32bit Embedded Hardware .

Summary: The Help menu in the Embedded MATLAB Editor has a broken link.
Description: Selecting Embedded MATLAB  in the Help menu of the Embedded MATLAB Editor opens a page that indicates the documentation cannot be found.
Workaround: The broken link is caused by a typographical error in the name of the HTML help file.  To reach help for Embedded MATLAB Function Blocks, follow these steps:Type doc Simulink on the MATLAB command line.Select Blocks - Alphabetical List in the Simulink documentation.In the list of blocks, search for Embedded MATLAB Function.(The title will be misspelled as GEmbedded MATLAB Function.)

Summary: Why do I get an error when I try to log to disk?
Description: When I configure a videoinput  object to use disk logging, I receive the error "Failed to make compressed stream. Unable to determine reason." For example: vid = videoinput('dcam',1,'Y8_640x480');vid.LoggingMode = 'disk';my_log = 'myfile1.avi';aviobj = avifile(my_log,'compression','none');vid.DiskLogger = aviobj;start(vid)Error event occurred at 08:13:40 for video input object: Y8_640x480-dcam-1.dcam: Error writing data to the DiskLogger file.Failed to make compressed stream. Unable to determine reason.??? dcam: Error writing data to the DiskLogger file.Failed to make compressed stream. Unable to determine reason.

Summary: Reading a comma-separated list from a memory-mapped file
Description: When you construct a memmapfile  object to map a file into memory, you can define the formatting of the data in the file by specifying a 'format'  parameter in the constructor call. The constructor then returns a memmapfile  object that partitions data read from the file into fields that are very similar to the fields of a MATLAB structure. In most cases, you can then read from the memory-mapped file as you would read from a MATLAB structure.For example,   % Map file records.dat into memory.   m = memmapfile('records.dat', 'offset', 5000, ...      'format', {'double', 1, 'f1'}, 'repeat', 5);   % Read from field f1 of element 2:   m.data(2).f1   ans =     1.5111e-094 You cannot, however, accurately use the following syntax to read from arange of indices into a comma-separated list as you can with a structure. As shown here, MATLAB returns only one value (that is m.data(2).f1 ) and not the full range specified:   m.data(2:4).f1   ans =     1.5111e-094 Note:  In R14SP2, the above statement returns only the   first value in the range and no error message.  In releases after   R14SP2, MATLAB issues an error message and returns no value.

Summary: Phase response in FVTool incorrect for frequency [-pi, pi)
Description: The phase response in FVTool is wrong when the Frequency Range is set to [-pi, pi).
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can specify a frequency vector instead having FVTool use NFFT.

Summary: Displaying signal storage class can crash Simulink
Description: Selecting Port/Signal Displays->Storage Class  from the model editor's Format  menu can, in some cases, cause Simulink to crash while updating a diagram. In particular, this can occur if the label associated with the output port of a nonvirtual, bus-capable block resolves to a custom data object.
Workaround: Turn off display of port signal class

Summary: Editing a block diagram with an open Scope window can hang MATLAB
Description: Editing a block label or annotation while a Scope window is open and then closing the Scope window causes the MATLAB desktop to become unresponsive.
