However, the Simulink editor remains responsive, allowing you to save your changes. Further, if you wait long enough, the MATLAB desktop may become responsive again. If not, you must kill and restart MATLAB.
Workaround: Close all Scope windows before editing a block label or annotation.

Summary: immovie cannot display indexed images correctly
Description: The immovie  function does not display indexed images correctly. For example, use the following code to create a movie: [X, map] = dicomread('US-PAL-8-10x-echo.dcm');mov = immovie(X, map); In this example, immovie  successfully creates the mov  variable. However, immovie  does not display the movie frames. Instead, immovie  displays only a single grayscale image.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the immovie function in your current installation with the updated version that is contained in the attached file.Rename the immovie.m file in the following directory in your existing MATLAB installation, giving it a name such as immovie.m.old. matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\immovie.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.Download the attached file, extract immovie.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: ERT 'Create Simulink (S-Function) block' option fails to compile for 'Define' and 'Const' CSC tunable parameters
Description: During ERT code generation with the Create Simulink (S-Function) block  option, if there are tunable parameters of the Define  or Const  predefined Simulink custom storage classes (CSCs), the generated code will not compile. By definition, these parameters areread-only and cannot be changed at runtime; however, the code generation process does not detect and report this condition.
Workaround: Select Ignore custom storage classes from the Real-Time Workshop pane of the ERT target options before generating the ERT S-function code.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler available on x86_64 platforms
Description: The printed "Release Notes for Release 14 with Service Pack 2" and the HTML version distributed on the R14SP2 CD contain a documentation error.This error has been corrected for the HTML and PDF versions of the release notes that appear on the MathWorks Web site. The "Supported Platforms" section incorrectly stated that the MATLAB Compiler is not available on x86_64 platforms. It is available on that platform for R14SP2.

Summary: Stateflow machine-parented data using alias types causes segmentation violations
Description: Stateflow machine-parented data using alias types causes segmentation violation at code generation.

Summary: Closing a Bus Selector dialog box can crash Simulink
Description: Opening and closing a Bus Selector dialog box whilesimulating a model can crash Simulink.

Summary: imnoise performs inappropriate rounding after scaling in 'Poisson' option
Description: imnoise  scales double-precision input images by 1e12 to get the desired Poisson distribution mean. After this scaling, the function inappropriately rounds so that Poisson distributions with fractional mean values cannot be simulated.

Summary: Incorrect code from a chart due to combination of address operation on input data and expression folding
Description: Stateflow Coder generates incorrect code like the following from a chart:   rtB.Out = (real_T)foo(&(rtU.In1 * 2.0));  // rtB.Out = (real_T)foo(&(rtU.In1) * 2.0)) is correct whereThe chart contains custom code and its arguments are passed via address operations like the following.{Out = foo(&In);}  The expression folding option folds the expression for In .
Workaround: To work around this issue, create more than one access to this input variable in the chart. For example:call_dummy_function(&x);y = foo(&x);The multiple accesses to the input variable will bypass the expression-folding optimization.

Summary: Automatic rate transition handling can cause incorrect results
Description: If the Automatically handle rate transition  option is on, Simulink may incorrectly insert a rate transition block between nonvirtual subsystems operating at different rates, leadingto incorrect results. This can happen when the nonvirtual subsystems are not at the model's root level and the subsystems themselves contain nonvirtual subsystems.

Summary: xPC Target Explorer cannot work with target applications that contain non-English strings
Description: xPC Target Explorer could not connect to a target PC if the downloaded target application was built from a model that contained non-English strings.

Summary: From block dialog box takes a long time to open
Description: The dialog box for the From block can take a longtime to open if the block is in a model that contains many links to library blocks. The reason is that the dialog box displays all tags for Goto blocks defined in the libraries referenced by a model and this requires opening all libraries referenced by the model and searching the libraries for the tags defined by Goto blocks in those libraries. This operation can take a long time.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Charts with boolean inputs generate incorrect code if the Use Strong Data Typing with Simulink IO property is not on
Description: Charts that meet the following: Use Strong Data Typing with Simulink IO property unset Boolean and uint8 types on either the input or output data  are likely to generate incorrect code for simulation when debugging is enabled.
Workaround: As a workaround, set to on the chart propery Use Strong Data Typing with Simulink IO. This enforces the actual data types on the Simulink boundary and eliminates the boolean and uint8 casts.

Summary: Alias types on Stateflow input data do not always work
Description: Alias types on Stateflow input data do not always work. They might generate a confusing error message during model compilation.

Summary: Using an Imaging Source 31F03 FireWire camera with the Image Acquisition Toolbox causes a segmentation violation
Description: When running the following code, where the winvideo device with ID 1 is an Imaging Source 31F03 FireWire camera, a segmentation violation occurs with the Image Acquisition Toolbox: vid = videoinput('winvideo', 1); start(vid)
Workaround: To workaround this problem, configure the Exposure property to a valid value before initiating an acquisition. Valid values can be determined using the propinfo function:vid = videoinput('winvideo', 1); src = getselectedsource(vid); propinfo(src, 'Exposure') set(src, 'Exposure', -1); start(vid)

Summary: Dfilt phasez and zerophase with 2-element frequency vectors causes error
Description: Passing a frequency vector of two elements to dfilt phasez  or  dfilt zerophase  produces an error.

Summary: Simulink allows a block to specify a bus object as its output data type
Description: Simulink allows a block to specify a bus object as the data type of its output even though buses are conceptually different from data types.  This is particularly confusing since the output is not a bus (e.g. you can not place a bus selector on it).

Summary: Erroneous result when retrieving String from multiline UIcontrol of style edit
Description: If you create a single-line edit  UIcontrol with an empty String  property and subsequently update it as a multiline edit  by setting Max - Min >1 , the String  property may not get updated when the user enters a string, and using a command such asget(handles.edit1,'String') may erroneously return an empty vector. Note that GUIDE creates a single-line edit  UIcontrol with an empty String  as a default.
Workaround: Programmatic UIcontrol creation. Create the UIcontrol initially with a valid String or with Max - Min > 1. GUIDE. There is no workaround at this time.

Summary: Converted models do not have enough sample-time precision
Description: The sl_convert_to_model_reference  command can fail to use enough precision to set the sample time on the root level inport and outport blocks for some models. As a result, a generated models may error out with an error message similar to: The sample time period (0.003333333) specified for 'MyModel/out1' is not an integer multiple of the fixed step size (0.003333333333333334) specified for model.
Workaround: To work around this issue in Release 14, Release 14 Service Pack 1, and Release 14 Service Pack 2, download the appropriate version of the attached file. Here are the required steps:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\simulink\simulink\private\subsystem_to_model_reference.m (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.) Rename the file to subsystem_to_model_reference.m.old.Download attachment_254955_20100720.zip and unzip it in a temp dir.Rename the appropriate version of subsystem_to_model_reference_<release>.m.to subsystem_to_model_reference.m and place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: Target preferences block does not check whether L2 cache and internal memory section(s) overlap
Description: Target preferences blocks do not test for memory space conflicts. L2 cache and some internal memory sections might attempt to use the same memory space, potentially causing a run-time crash. You can use the memory configurations option for ISRAM in the target preferences block to modify the ISRAM and L2 cache size. When you enable L2 cache, a portion of the ISRAM is set aside as L2 cache. ISRAM length should therefore be reduced accordingly to account for the L2 memory dedicated for cache usage.
Workaround: When you enable and use the L2 cache option in a Target Preferences block, reduce the size of the ISRAM memory allocation by the size of the L2 cache.For example, if you use a 128Kb L2 cache option with DM642 EVM Target Preferences block, reduce the ISRAM length from the default 0x00040000 (256Kb) size to 0x00020000 (128Kb).

Summary: Simulink models containing a STOP block might fail to honor stopping conditions
Description: Simulink models that contain a Stop block fail to honor the stopping condition when you set the TI C6000 compiler optimization level to Local(-o1) , Function(-o2) , or File(-o3) .
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by setting the compiler optimization level to None.To change the compiler optimization setting, follow these steps in the configuration parameters for your model.Select Simulation->Configuration Parameters from the model menu bar.On the Select tree, choose TI C6000 Compiler. The Complier options become available in the right pane.Under Compiler, set Optimization level to None.Click OK to close the dialog and save your changes.Now rebuild your model and regenerate the code. The newly generated code now honors your stop conditions.

Summary: Cannot run compiled application with classes referenced only as strings
Description: MATLAB Compiler generates incorrect standalone applications that have classes defined only in the properties of a class. This problem occurs when a file refers to a class name as a string but not through a function call. This command generates a standalone application for an example program named doclass.m  that refers to a class name only as a string:  mcc -N -m doclass The resulting executable file results in runtime errors: Warning: an error occurred while parsing class mypackage.myparent: Invalid data type. Warning: an error occurred while parsing class mypackage.myclass: Invalid superclass handle. Error in ==> doclass at 2  Unable to create a class array for class mypackage.myclass MATLAB:dispatcher:opaqueClassCreationFailure  This problem occurs in some toolboxes and results in similar runtime errors for compiled applications that call functions defined in those toolboxes.

Summary: On UNIX systems, Stateflow can become inoperable if the rm command is aliased
Description: On UNIX systems, it is normal to alias rm  and mv  to rm -i  and mv -i , respectively. However, these aliases can cause Stateflow to become inoperable. In particular, these commands can cause Stateflow to hang while cleaning the code-generation project directory.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can start MATLAB from a command shell where the commands rm and mv are not aliased.

Summary: Breakpoint Cannot be Set in Shadowed File
Description: If you try to use the GUI to set a breakpoint in a MATLAB file opened in the Editor/Debugger, the following message appears under certain conditions: Error using ==> dbstop This error is incorrect. It occurs when the numeric format is set to hex , bank , or + , and when another file with the same name is open, which is shadowing the file in which you want to set the breakpoint.
Workaround: Change the format in MATLAB to something other than hex, bank, or + (for example, to short), and then close and reopen the file in the Editor/Debugger. If you then try to set a breakpoint, a dialog appears stating that the file is shadowed by another function on the MATLAB path.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler segmentation faults after clear all
Description: The MATLAB Compiler, running in MATLAB, sometimes generates a segmentation violation after a clear all  command. For example:>> mcc -Nmv doclass.m >> clear all >> mcc -Nmv doclass.m   The second invocation of mcc  may generate a segmentation fault.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Run the MATLAB Compiler outside of MATLAB.Do not use the clear all command in MATLAB between runs of the MATLAB Compiler.

Summary: Use of .wave command in HDL Filter Link demo causes errors from ModelSim 6.0 and above
Description: The HDL Filter Link demo (hdlfilterlink ) uses the Link for ModelSim as a testbench for generated HDL for a digital filter. This demo uses the ModelSim .wave  command to zoom the waveform viewing window to full scale.  However, since the .wave  command was deprecated by Model Technology starting in v 6.0 of ModelSim, the demo causes a script error in ModelSim.
Workaround: To work around the problem, edit the MATLAB function hdlfilterlinkcmds.m and remove the bad command ('.wave zoomfull').

Summary: freqz2 returns wrong answer for large filter sizes and user-provided frequency vectors
Description: If you call the freqz2  function with a filter of dimension greater than 64 and with user-specified frequency vectors, freqz2  ignores the user-specified frequency vectors and instead computes the result based on 64-by-64 equal spacing.

Summary: Loop rolling: bad integer type used for big vectors
Description: Under certain conditions, Real-Time Workshop generates a loop index variable that overflows. This can cause a program to deadlock or loop indefinitely. For example, if your model is configured for a generic 16-bit processor, Real-Time Workshop produces the following code:     typedef int int16_T;    int16_T i1;     /* Sum: '/Sum' incorporates:      *  Inport: '/In1'      */     rtb_Sum = 1.0 + idx_test_U.In1;     /* Outport: '/Out1' incorporates:      *  Gain: '/Gain'      */     for (i1 = 0; i1 < 100000; i1++) {       idx_test_Y.Out1[i1] = rtb_Sum * (idx_test_ConstP.Gain_Gain[i1]);     }  The value 100000 overflows the 16-bit variable i1 .

Summary: Subsystem builds: generated file names mangled when right-click build used
Description: During a right-click subsystem build, Real-Time Workshop mangles generated file names to create unique file names if it detects a name conflict with any existing Models MATLAB code files Built-in functions Generated executable files

Summary: Building xPC Target ERT models that contain Signal Processing Blockset blocks does not work correctly
Description: Building xPC Target ERT models that contain Signal Processing Blockset blocks fails. xPC Target cannot load the target application on the target PC.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Stop MATLAB.Rename the following file to rtwmakecfg.m.old: matlabroot\toolbox\dspblks\dspmex\rtwmakecfg.mDownload the attached file and place it in the same directory mentioned in Step 2.Restart MATLAB.In the MATLAB Command Window, type: rehash toolboxcache Rebuild your model.

Summary: Documentation for "Customizing Plots from the Command Line" inaccessible
Description: In the Control System Toolbox documentation shipped with the product, the section entitled "Customizing Plots from the Command Line" under "Customization" is inaccessible.
Workaround: To view this documentation, either Use the Control System Toolbox User's Guide PDFSee https://www.mathworks.com/help/releases/R2022a/control/analysis-plot-customization.html.

Summary: Non-US Keyboard on Windows: Cannot Enter Characters Like Caret (^) and Tilde (~)
Description: When using some non-US keyboards on Windows platforms, you cannot enter characters that require an AltGr  and dead key combination. The caret (^) and tilde (~) frequently use this key combination, but other characters might be affected too.
Workaround: These are some workarounds:You can use another application to enter the character, and then cut and paste the character into the MATLAB component.Use a function instead, if possible. For example, use the power function instead of the caret (^) key, and use the not function instead of the tilde (~) key.For editing, use an editor other than the one provided with MATLAB. Select File > Preferences > Editor/Debugger and specify a different text editor.If you are using R14SP2, you can install the attached patch file by performing these steps:Download the attached patchaltgr.txt file.Rename the file to patchAltGr.zip.Copy patchAltGr.zip to matlabroot\java, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed. You can determine the pathname by running the matlabroot function.Start MATLAB and runcd([matlabroot '/java'])mkdir('patch')unzip('patchAltGr.zip')Exit MATLAB.Start MATLAB. You should now be able to enter all characters, such as the tilde (~).

Summary: Dialog box text is sometimes truncated at the top
Description: In some cases, text in dialog boxes in MATLAB or any MathWorks product appears truncated at the top. One example of when this can happen is if you have Adobe software installed that uses Adobe Helvetica fonts.
Workaround: In most cases, this workaround corrects the problem. Run:set(0,'DefaultUicontrolFontname','Sans Serif');To automatically execute this statement every time you start MATLAB, include the statement in your startup.m file. For more information about creating a startup.m file, run the following in MATLABdocsearch 'startup options'

Summary: Dragging lines is sluggish on Windows with NVidia driver
Description: On a Windows system with an nVidia Quadro FX 500 video card using the latest drivers, it is nearly impossible to connect blocks by dragging lines because the lines either do not appear or move around unpredictably.

Summary: United Electronic Industries (UEI) PD2-AO (96 Channels) does not work with random channel specifications
Description: The UEI PD2-AO 96 channel board only works if you specify a contiguous group of channels. These channels also must be listed in order. If you enter a non-contiguous channel list in the Channel vector  parameter, no output will be seen from the board.For example, if the channel list is: [1 3 5] no output will be seen.
Workaround: As a workaround, if you change the specfication to: [1 2 3 4 5]the output will be seen on all five channels. You can attach a ground or other appropriate signal to the channels that you do not want to use (in this example, 2 and 4).

Summary: United Electronic Industries (UEI) PD2-AO series driver blocks do not display analog output
Description: There is a bug in the UEI PD2-AO series driver blocks for 8, 16, and 32 analog output channels. This bug prevents analog output from working on any channel. No workarounds exist.

Summary: Selecting sample-time independence for a model can trigger misleading warnings
Description: Selecting a fixed-step solver and setting the solver's Periodic sample time constraint  to Ensure sample time independent  can cause misleading warnings similar to the following: Warning: Source 'm1/Uniform Random Number' specifies that its sample time (-1) should be back-inherited. You should explicitly specify the sample time of sources.  If you take the corrective action suggested by the warning, updating the model causes an error.

Summary: Measurement Computing USB-1616FS and PMD FS series devices generate message "MCC: Count must be an integer multiple of packetsize for recycle mode"
Description: Due to changes in the Instacal driver starting with Version 5.55, you might receive the following message when using the USB-1616FS or PMD FS series device:  MCC: Count must be an integer multiple of packetsize for recycle mode This has been identified as an incompatibility between the Data Acquisition Toolbox and the Measurement Computing Instacal driver.
Workaround: A possible workaround is to use the attached version of setBufferForPMD.m and change the configuration file for your device. To change the configuration of the device:Quit MATLAB.Change to the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private, where $MATLAB is the directory where you installed MATLAB.Back up the file mwmcc.ini to a another name such as mwmcc.bak.Open mwmcc.ini and find the entry for your device, such as [PMD-1608FS].In that section, find the line starting withPacketSize =, and delete it.Save and close the file.Restart MATLAB.You must call setBufferForPMD with your analog input object just before calling start.

Summary: Cannot overwrite block from Filter Wizard or block command in masked subsystem
Description: If you try to overwrite a Simulink block generated with the block  command or the Filter Wizard GUI, an error occurs if the target block is located in a masked subsystem.

Summary: Changing signal dimensions can cause a segV
Description: The following sequence of operations can cause a memory segmentation violation (segV) when updating a block diagram  containing bus signals and bus selector blocks: Update the block diagram. Change the dimensions of some signals. Update the diagram.
Workaround: Save and close the model after changing a model setting. Then reopen the model and do the remaing operations.

Summary: List box performance issue
Description: Resetting the String  property of a list box containing more than 100 elements, may take a significantly longer time than for a list box with fewer elements.
Workaround: Temporarily change the list box to a push button, reset the String property of the control, and then change the control back to a list box. The following code illustrates this procedure:% h is the handle to the listbox uicontrolset(h,'Visible','off','Style','Pushbutton');set(h,'String',New_String);set(h,'Visible','on','Style','Listbox');

Summary: Documentation: parameters missing from configurartion parameters reference table
Description: The following parameters are missing from and should be added to the table in the "Configuration Parameters Reference" appendix in the      Real-Time Workshop documentation:  TargetTypeEmulationWarnSuppressLevel - When greater than or equal to 2, suppresses warning messages that Real-Time Workshop displays when emulating integer sizes in rapid prototyping environments    TargetPreprocMaxBitsSint - Specifies the maximum number of bits that the target C preprocessor is capable of using for signed integer math  TargetPreprocMaxBitsUint - Specifies the maximum number of bits that the target C preprocessor is capable of using for unsigned integer math  Note that these parameters have no graphical user interface equivalent.

Summary: Using ellip function with MATLAB Compiler causes errors
Description: Using the MATLAB Complier to compile a function that calls the ellip  function produces errors, such as the following:Undefined command/function 'kratio'.MATLAB:UndefinedFunction
Workaround: To work around this issue, include the functions being used by FMINSEARCH as arguments to MCC. These functions are KRATIO and VRATIO.

Summary: Microcontroller mode only supported
Description: The documentation for the Embedded Target for TI C2000 incorrectly states that microprocessor mode is supported.

Summary: fgoalattain and fseminf functions error on problems with nonlinear equality constraints when both objective and constraint gradients are supplied
Description: Because the Jacobian of a transformed problem that is passed to the optimization algorithm is not set up properly, fgoalattain  and fseminf  error on problems with nonlinear equality constraints when providing both the constraint and objective gradients.

Summary: Treat as atomic unit check box not checked for inherently atomic subsystems
Description: If you create a subsystem from scratch that is atomic by nature, e.g., a triggered subsystem, Simulink disables but does not check the Treat as atomic unit  check box on the subsystem's dialog box. For example, suppose that you create a Subsystem block and open its parameter dialog box. The Treat as atomic unit  appears unchecked and enabled on the dialog box. Now suppose you close the dialog box, drop a Trigger block into the subsystem, and reopen the dialog box. The Treat as atomic unit  check box now correctly appears greyed out because a triggered subsystem is atomic by nature. However, the check box is not checked, suggesting, incorrectly, that the subsystem is not atomic. This problem affects other types of subsystems that are atomic by nature, including Enabled, Iterator, and If Action subsystems.
Workaround: Check the subsystem's Treat as atomic unit option before  adding the Enable or Trigger block to the subsystem.

Summary: sparse iterative methods with funs with certain trailing parameters
Description: The nine sparse iterative solvers (PCG, BICG, BICGSTAB, CGS, GMRES, LSQR, MINRES, SYMMLQ, and QMR) can accept as an argument a matrix A or a function AFUN that performs Y=AFUN(X) => Y=A*X. In addition, if AFUN has trailing parameters, e.g., Y=AFUN(X,P1,P2,...,PN), you can pass those parameters to the call as shown here:   X = PCG(AFUN,B,TOL,MAXIT,M1,M2,X0,P1,P2,...,PN) For the 6 sparse iterative methods (PCG, BICGSTAB, CGS, GMRES, MINRES, and SYMMLQ) that do not require AFUN to be of the form Y=AFUN(X,TRANS_FLAG), if the final parameter PN is a struct, a Java object, or an OOPS object, then the iterative method will fail with an error similar to the one shown here:    >> x = pcg(afun,b,tol,maxit,M1,M2,x0,P1)   ??? Error using ==> sparfun\private\iterapp   user supplied function ==> afun   failed with the following error:   Function 'strcmp' is not defined for values of class 'struct'.
Workaround: Replace the use of trailing parameters P1,P2,...,PN as shown:   X = PCG(AFUN,B,TOL,MAXIT,M1,M2,X0,P1,P2,...,PN)with an anonymous function wrapping AFUN:   X = PCG(@(x)AFUN(x,P1,P2,...,PN),B,TOL,MAXIT,M1,M2,X0)Note: this is the preferred syntax for using all "function functions" starting in R14.If you do not wish to change your calling code and you have write permissions in your MATLAB install area, you can edit the file private/iterapp.m and replace the 3 instances of strcmp in eitherstrcmp(varargin{end},'transp')orstrcmp(varargin{end},'notransp')withisequal(varargin{end},'transp')orisequal(varargin{end},'notransp')

Summary: Availability of the CAN Message Blocks infrastructure (including CANdb support) in Real-Time Workshop when no Embedded Target products are installed
Description: The CAN Message Blocks infrastructure (including CANdb support) is provided with Real-Time Workshop 6.2 (Release 14SP2). However, this functionality is not available to Real-Time Workshop users if none of The MathWorks' Embedded Target products, for example, Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555, are installed. Due to a bug in Real-Time Workshop 6.2, the directory containing the CAN library blocks is missing from the MATLAB search path.
Workaround: The CAN Message Blocks library can be made available to users of Real-Time Workshop 6.2 (Release 14SP2) who do not have any of The MathWorks? Embedded Target products installed. Note that this workaround applies only to the CAN Message Blocks library, and not to the CAN Drivers (Vector) library, which is available only with the installation of an Embedded Target product from The MathWorks.To gain access to the CAN Message Blocks library, use the following steps:Add the CAN Message Blocks directory to the MATLAB search path by issuing the following command at the MATLAB prompt: addpath([matlabroot '\toolbox\rtw\targets\common\can\blocks']) Configure the CAN Blockset Reference documentation so that it appears under the Real-Time Workshop documentation in the MATLAB Help browser: Close MATLAB.Rename the file matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\rtw\info.xml(where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt) to info.xml.old. Download the file info.xml, listed below under Attachments. Place the new file in the same directory as the file you renamed.Add a CAN Message Blocks entry to the Simulink Library Browser by downloading the file slblocks.m, listed below under Attachments, and placing it in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\common\can\blocks\(where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt).Restart MATLAB.After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Simulink data type objects: use of HeaderFile property when generating S-Function for a subsystem can produce unexpected results
Description: You can specify a legacy header file for a Simulink data type object (Simulink.AliasType , Simulink.NumericType , or Simulink.Bus ) by using the HeaderFile  property. If you do, Simulink includes the data type definition for the object from the specified header file instead of generating one. If the legacy header file specifies a memory alignment that differs from what Simulink expects, functional problems arise if the data type crosses the S-Function interface between Simulink and the generated code. Consider the following example: You use a legacy header file to define the data type for a structure that crosses the interface between an S-function generated for a subsystem and the rest of a Simulink model. The definition in the legacy header file uses bit-packing to store that structure.   The resulting memory misalignment can cause the S-function to behave unexpectedly.
Workaround: When generating code for a subsystem, clear the HeaderFile property for Simulink.DataType properties used. You can restore  the property after code generation.

Summary: createClassFromWsdl: example service no longer supported
Description: In the help and reference page for the function createClassFromWsdl , the example returns a value of -1  because the xmethods.net  service is no longer supporting that example.

Summary: Identifiers: clashes not reported when global data store memory is defined by Simulink.Signal object with alias
Description: You can use Simulink.Signal  objects to define global data stores, and you can specify the objects with an alias (RTWInfo.Alias  property) that Real-Time Workshop is to use as the data store identifier in the generated code. When generating code, Real-Time Workshop needs to ensure that the identifier used for a global data store does not conflict with other global identifiers (for example, identifiers for tunable parameters or global signals). However, the current check for symbol clashes unconditionally uses data store names as the identifiers, even if you set aliases.   Consider the following model scenario: The model uses a tunable parameter P .The model also uses a global data store A .A  is defined by a Simulink.Signal  object for which the RTWInfo.Alias  property is set to P . In this case, Real-Time Workshop does not report a symbol clash because it uses the actual name of the global data store unconditionally to check for identifier conflicts, instead of using the alias that is set.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Cannot select bus signal originating in a referenced model
Description: Simulink displays a nonexistant signal error if you try to update a model that selects a signal from a virtual bus under the following circumstances:  The virtual bus's source is a Model block. The signal's label is propagated from the Model block.
Workaround: Set the name of the signal in the parent model to be the name of the corresponding signal in the child model.

Summary: Solaris: MATLAB Fails to Start When Stack Size Is Unlimited
Description: When you try to start MATLAB on Solaris and the machine's default stack size is set to unlimited, MATLAB fails to start and this exception appears: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: unable to create new native thread To verify that this is the cause of the problem, run the following at an xterm prompt: ulimit -a (For some UNIX shells, the command is limit  or unlimit  rather than ulimit . For more information, run man ulimit .) The limits for various OS parameters appear, including the stack size limit. If the stack size limit value is set to unlimited, MATLAB will not start.
Workaround: You can fix the problem for just MATLAB or more generally.MATLAB specific fix: Create a file named java.opts in the directory from which MATLAB is started. Put the following line in the file: -XX:ThreadStackSize=8100When you start MATLAB, it loads the JVM with a default stack size limit that provides approximately 8 MB for each new thread.General fix (might affect other applications): Use the ulimit command to adjust the system's default stack size to something other than unlimited.

Summary: Spaces in MATLAB install directory name cause vsim and Link for ModelSim demos to fail
Description: If the name of the directory in which MATLAB is installed contains spaces, the Link for ModelSim vsim  command fails. Link for ModelSim demos will also fail.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, you must upgrade to ModelSim version 6.0b (or higher). This version of ModelSim fixes a problem handling spaces in directory names. In addition to upgrading Modelsim, you must apply the Link for ModelSim patches attached to this bug report (see Attachments below), as follows:Extract the patch files to a temporary directory. To do this on Windows, you can either run the self- extracting archive lfmpatch.exe or unzip lfmpatch.zip. On Linux, use the command unzip lfmpatch.zip.Copy vsim.m from your temporary directory to MATLABROOT\toolbox\modelsim\modelsim, replacing the existing file.Copy the other files from your temporary directory to MATLABROOT\toolbox\modelsim\modelsimdemos, replacing the existing files.

Summary: Storing a filter in the filter manager causes generated MATLAB code file to be empty
Description: If you store a filter in the filter manager and then try to generate a MATLAB code file, the generated file will be empty.

Summary: Preferences Directory Sometimes Becomes Corrupt
Description: In some situations, files in the preferences directory become corrupt, for example, when running multiple versions of MATLAB. This can cause MATLAB to become nonresponsive.
Workaround: R13 (MATLAB Version 6.5) to R14SP2 (MATLAB Version 7.0.4)If you are running R13 through R14SP2, perform the following steps:Run prefdir in the Command Window to determine where the MATLAB preferences directory is located on your system. MATLAB returns, for exampleC:\WINNT\Profiles\{username}\Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R14which is the preference directory for all R14 releases on Windowsor\home\{username}\.matlab\R13which is the preferences directory for all R13 releases on UNIX.Quit MATLAB.Rename the preferences directory for your current release and any older releases.  For example, for R14 (MATLAB 7.0), rename the directory from R14 to R14old. If you also have R13 (MATLAB 6.5) installed, rename the R13 directory to R13old.Start MATLAB. This creates a new preferences directory with default settings for the release you are running, for example R14.This should resolve the problem of corrupt preferences. If it does not solve the problems as you expected, you can restore your original preferences directory by deleting the newly created directories, R14 and R13, and renaming R14old to R14 and R13old to R13.R14SP3 (MATLAB Version 7.1)Starting in R14SP3, each release has its own preferences directory. This new structure is expected to resolve preference corruption problems. You can restore R14SP3 preference settings to either the MathWorks defaults for the release, or to your settings from the previous R14 release  Use MathWorks Default SettingsRun prefdir in the Command Window to determine where the MATLAB preferences directory is located on your system. MATLAB returns, for exampleC:\WINNT\Profiles\{username}\Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R14SP3for Windows, or\home\{username}\.matlab\R14SP3for UNIX.Quit MATLAB.Rename the R14SP3 preferences directory, for example,  to R14SP3old.In that directory (the one containing R14sp3old), create a new, empty directory named R14SP3.Start MATLAB. MATLAB assumes it is running a new installation and populates the preferences directory with the MathWorks default preference settings for the release. Migrate Preferences from Previous R14 ReleasesRun prefdir in the Command Window to determine where the MATLAB preferences directory is located on your system. MATLAB returns, for exampleC:\WINNT\Profiles\{username}\Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R14SP3for Windows, or\home\{username}\.matlab\R14SP3for UNIX.Quit MATLAB.Delete the R14SP3 preferences directory.Start MATLAB. MATLAB assumes it is running on a system that just upgraded to R14SP3 and populates the R14SP3 preferences directory with the preferences from the R14 preference directory.

Summary: Inline invariant signals: storage generated when option is selected
Description: If an invariant signal is the input for a For Iterator or While Iterator block and you select the Inline invariant signals  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, Real-Time Workshop might not honor the option setting and generate storage for some inline invariant signals.

Summary: Find Files Tool Results can be Incorrect with Match Case Option
Description: In the Find Files tool, if you search for a filename containing uppercase letters and the Match case  option is selected, no results are returned even though matching files might exist.
Workaround: Clear the Match case option to find filenames that contain uppercase letters.

Summary: Kinematics mode simulation of closed-loop machines: segmentation violation
Description: Certain closed-loop mechanisms could produce a segmentation violation when simulated in Kinematics mode. This happens when the cut joint is any of the following: A Six-DoFThe Six-DoF inside a Body Spring-DamperA Custom Joint with 3 prismatic and 3 revolute primitives
Workaround: To work around this issue, try either of the following:In a loop in Kinematics mode, choose a Joint that does not have six DoFs as the preferred cut joint.  For example, choose a Joint that is not a Bushing, Six-DoF, or a Custom Joint with 3 prismatics and 3 revolutes.Replace any Six-DoF joint with a Joint block that has fewer DoFs.

Summary: Forward compatibility issue with certain blocks
Description: Modifying a model containing any of the following blocks in Simulink 6.3 will result in bad links when opening the model in previous Simulink releases:AWGN Channel Binary Symmetric Channel Multipath Rayleigh Fading Channel Rician Fading Channel Voltage-Controlled Oscillator All blocks from the AM and PM sublibraries of the Digital Baseband Modulation library

Summary: Icons are missing from Root Locus Property Editor Limit Stack
Description: The Limits  pane of the Root Locus Property Editor contains a  Limit Stack  section. The icons are missing from the buttons within  this section.
Workaround: To use these buttons, position your mouse over each one and  a brief description of the action each button performs appears directly  below.

Summary: MATLAB crashes evaluating a recursive function call
Description: In rare cases, an expression containing a recursive function call combined with element-by-element array functions (e.g., +, -, .*) can cause MATLAB to segv.
Workaround: Type 'feature accel off' at the MATLAB command prompt to disable the accelerator.

Summary: realizemdl/Filter Wizard errors the first time the command is run
Description: Filter Wizard (or the realizemdl  command) may produce an error if the Signal Processing Blockset is not installed. This error occurs if Simulink has not been previously loaded because the Integer Delay block can not be found.
Workaround: Start Simulink before using the realizemdl command.

Summary: Measurement Computing Switch and Sense 8/8, USB-SSR24, USB-SSR08, USB-ERB24, USB-ERB08, and USB-PDISO8 devices not supported
Description: The Data Acquisition Toolbox does not support the Measurement Computing Switch and Sense 8/8, USB-SSR24, USB-SSR08, USB-ERB24, USB-ERB08, or USB-PDISO8 devices.  The complete list of supported devices is available from:https://www.mathworks.com/hardware-support/data-acquistion-software.html
Workaround: For the workaround to support these devices with the Data Acquisition Toolbox, use the following steps:  1. Copy the file     $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private/mwmcc.ini    (where $MATLAB = the MATLAB root directory on your machine) to $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private/mwmcc.ini.old  2. Use a text editor to add the following lines to the bottom of the original mwmcc.ini file: ;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;[Switch and Sense 8/8]ID = 0x84Bus = USBDIOConfigurable = 0DPORTS = 2DPORT0LINES = 8DPORT0TYPE = 1DPORT0CONFIG = 0DPORT1LINES = 8DPORT1TYPE = 1DPORT1CONFIG = 1;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;[USB-SSR24]ID = 0x85Bus = USBDIOConfigurable = 1DPORTS = 4DPORT0LINES = 4DPORT0TYPE = 12DPORT0CONFIG = 2DPORT1LINES = 4DPORT1TYPE = 13DPORT1CONFIG = 2DPORT2LINES = 8DPORT2TYPE = 10DPORT2CONFIG = 2DPORT3LINES = 8DPORT3TYPE = 11DPORT3CONFIG = 2;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;[USB-SSR08]ID = 0x86Bus = USBDIOConfigurable = 1DPORTS = 2DPORT0LINES = 4DPORT0TYPE = 12DPORT0CONFIG = 2DPORT1LINES = 4DPORT1TYPE = 13DPORT1CONFIG = 2;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;[USB-ERB24]ID = 0x8aBus = USBDIOConfigurable = 1DPORTS = 4DPORT0LINES = 4DPORT0TYPE = 12DPORT0CONFIG = 2DPORT1LINES = 4DPORT1TYPE = 13DPORT1CONFIG = 2DPORT2LINES = 8DPORT2TYPE = 10DPORT2CONFIG = 2DPORT3LINES = 8DPORT3TYPE = 11DPORT3CONFIG = 2;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;[USB-ERB08]ID = 0x8bBus = USBDIOConfigurable = 1DPORTS = 2DPORT0LINES = 4DPORT0TYPE = 12DPORT0CONFIG = 2DPORT1LINES = 4DPORT1TYPE = 13DPORT1CONFIG = 2;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;;[USB-PDISO8]ID = 0x8cBus = USBDIOConfigurable = 0DPORTS = 2DPORT0LINES = 8DPORT0TYPE = 1DPORT0CONFIG = 0DPORT1LINES = 8DPORT1TYPE = 1DPORT1CONFIG = 13. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Enable Java Figures on Macintosh
Description: In Bioinformatics Toolbox 2.0.1 the functions seqlogo , seqshowwords , seqshoworfs ,and showalignment  use Java based figures. Currently onthe Macintosh, Java figures are not enabled by default.
Workaround: If you use these functions on a Macintosh, you should start MATLAB withmatlab -useJavaFiguresSome functionality does not work with modal windows. For example runninga Bioinformatics Toolbox demo opens a blank dialog box. A work aroundis to minimize and maximize the dialog box to force the window to refresh.

Summary: SimMechanics CAD translator does not work with SolidWorks 2005
Description: The previous version of the SolidWorks CAD translator for SimMechanics was 1.0.3.  It is compatible with SolidWorks 2003 and 2004.When used with SolidWorks 2005, this version either crashes or produces ill-formed SimMechanics models.

Summary: CANdb Packing / Unpacking blocks fail to read CANdb DBC files that contain large numerical values
Description: When using the CANdb Packing / Unpacking blocks, CANdb DBC files that contain CANdb Signal properties, minimum, maximum, offset or factor, with values that are outside the range of a signed 32-bit integer value do not parse correctly.   An example of an out of range value is 4211081215.

Summary: Tab Completion Uses First Match Instead of Narrowing List of Possible Completions
Description: In previous versions of MATLAB, when you press Tab  in the Command Window to show a list of partial completions, then type additional characters and press Tab  again, MATLAB narrows the list of completions. MATLAB no longer does this but instead completes the name using the first item in the list of possible completions. The previous behavior was more convenient because you could avoid scrolling through a long list to select an item.

Summary: Dependency Report Sometimes Fails to Show Child and Parent Functions
Description: In some situations, when you select the Dependency Report in the Current Directory browser and select the check boxes for Show child functions  or Show parent functions , then run the report, the report does not show any parent or child functions even though they exist. This happens when you are running MATLAB using a UNC path name (that is, one that starts like this \\drive-name\dir1\dir2\ ...).
Workaround: You can still use the Dependency Report if you make sure and run MATLAB from a letter drive (that is, one that starts like this C:\Work\Ami\matlab rather than \\drive-name\dir1\dir2\ ...).

Summary: mapprofile
Description: Function mapprofile  errors if the map axes contain a surfacerendered by the function geoshow .
Workaround: If you are using a version of Mapping Toolbox prior to 2.2, use the function meshm to render the surface rather than geoshow.

Summary: Atomic subsystems: incorrect code generated for Async Interrupt block
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates incorrect code for an atomic subsystem when both of the following conditions exist: The option RTW system code  is set to Function . The subsystem contains an Async Interrupt block.
Workaround: Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc to matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc.old.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\subsystemlib.tlc tomatlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\subsystemlib.tlc.old.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\subsystm.tlc tomatlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\subsystm.tlc.old.Download the attached files and place them in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mwRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Cannot read version 1.3 GSHHS data files
Description: An error results when using function gshhs  to readVersion 1.3 GSHHS data files. GSHHS Version 1.3 was introducedin September 2004. Data files from prior versions of GSHHSare read without error.

Summary: Stale custom code information in invisible Real-Time Workshop target causes code-generation errors
Description: Stale custom code information that was entered for a Stateflow simulation target was unintentionally copied to the invisible Real-Time Workshop target maintained by Stateflow. This invisible target is only an anchor for Stateflow code generation. However, Stateflow mistakenly used the custom code settings from this object, causing unexpected error messages.
Workaround: For releases prior to R14SP3, use the attached files as follows:Download the attached zip file to your local disk and unzip it.Copy new_target.m and sf_rtw.m to the directory$MATLAB/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/private.(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.)Copy get_custom_code_settings.m to the directory$MATLAB/toolbox/stateflow/coder/private.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: The -port flag does not work for startjobmanager and startworker
Description: The -port  flag for the startjobmanager  andstartworker  scripts doesnot work in MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine.
Workaround: If you must use ports other than the defaults, you can specify the range of usable port numbers by settingvalues for the MIN_MDCE_PORT and MAX_MDCE_PORTparameters in the mdce_def.sh or mdce_def.batfile.  For more information on setting the parameters in this file,see "Customizing Engine Services" in the MATLAB Distributed Computing  Engine Network Administrator's Guide.

Summary: mdistort
Description: Contour labels created by the function mdistort  are not visible.
Workaround: To work around this problem, execute the command zdatam(handlem('DISTORTMtext'),max(get(gca,'ZLim')))

Summary: Clock enable output of FIRDECIM filters does not work correctly
Description: The clock enable output of FIRDECIM filters does not work correctly when the Add pipeline registers  option is selected.  The input to the clock enable delay line is connected to the wrong signal.

Summary: Port name clashes are resolved differently in the generated filter code and testbench code
Description: The same name can be assigned to more than one port (via either the HDL Options  dialog or the command line property-value pairs). Filter Design HDL Coder resolves such name conflicts, ensuring that all port names are unique to produce valid HDL code. In previous releases, the way in which such unique port names were generated was not consistent between the generated HDL code and the generated test bench HDL code. This could lead to name mismatches that caused test benches to drive the generated filter HDL code incorrectly.
Workaround: In general, you should be careful to use unique names for all the ports in your designs.

Summary: Multicast fails when different users run job manager and client on same Macintosh
Description: Running a job manager and a client as different users on the same Macintosh machine causes multicast lookups to fail.
Workaround: To work around this problem, use unicast to access the job manager running on this machine. For example,    findResource('jobmanager','LookupURL','localhost')An alternative workaround is to run the job manager and client as the same user.  To simultaneously run the Distributed Computing Toolbox and MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine entities (job managers or workers) on a single Macintosh machine:Shut down all MDCE entities on the machine with mdce stopDelete all the log and checkpoint files associated with the MDCE entities on this machine.Add a file called java.opts to the directory MATLABROOT/toolbox/distcomp/bin.Edit this new java.opts file to include the following line:-Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=trueSave the java.opts file.Restart the MDCE services (mdce, job manager, worker) that you want running on this machine.

Summary: Cannot run client and worker sessions simultaneously on the same Macintosh
Description: Running the MDCE worker and the DCT client as the same user on the same Macintosh does not work. The worker session will hang when it begins to execute tasks.
Workaround: Because of this limitation on the Macintosh, the following workaround is necessary to simultaneously run the Distributed Computing Toolbox and MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine entities (job managers or workers) on a single Macintosh machine: Shut down all MDCE entities on the machine with     mdce stopDelete all the log and checkpoint files associated with the MDCE entities on this machine.Add a file called java.opts to the directory MATLABROOT/toolbox/distcomp/bin.Edit this new java.opts file to include the following line:    -Djava.awt.headless=trueSave the java.opts file.Restart the MDCE services (mdce, job manager, worker) that you want running on this machine.

Summary: makecform errors with v.4 ICC profile
Description: The makecform  function, when used with the makecform('icc', SrcProfile, DstProfile)  syntax, fails for certain ICC Version 4 profiles. The error message displayed is "Index exceeds matrix dimensions."
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the makecform function in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file: matlabroot/toolbox/images/images/makecform.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: makecform.m.OLDDownload the attached file, extract makecform.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: dfilt isstable for second-order objects may give the wrong answer
Description: The dfilt isstable  method sometimes returns the wrong answer for second-order IIR digital filter objects.
Workaround: Appending a zero to the denominator fixes the problem.

Summary: Clicking Help for SOS View Settings in FVTool generates an error
Description: When you click Help  for more information about the SOS View Settings in FVTool, you get an error.

Summary: Code for Custom Code Block: when used in an atomic subsystem generated code for block can appear twice
Description: When you include a Custom Code block in an atomic subsystem, code for the block appears twice in the generated code if the subsystem uses an input or output port with a direct feedthrough connection.
Workaround: If you are using Real-Time Workshop R14SP2, download the attached TLC files as follows:Identify your MATLAB root directory by entering the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:matlabrootQuit MATLAB.Rename the following TLC files to filename.tlc.old, where matlabroot is your MATLAB root directory:matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\lib\syslib.tlcmatlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\hookslib.tlcmatlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlcDownload the attached files to the directories indicated in step 3.Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcacheIf you are using a release prior to R14SP2, there is no workaround.

Summary: Referencing models containing Stateflow charts requires the Real-Time Workshop
Description: Referencing a model containing a Stateflow chart in another model causes a TLC error if the Real-Time Workshop is not installed.
Workaround: For a workaround for releases that precede the fix for this problem, go to www.mathworks.com, select Support, and search for solution 1-YCPFP.

Summary: Data Field of memmapfile Class Cannot Return a Comma-Separated List
Description: The Data  field of the memmapfile  class acts similarly to a MATLAB structure if the Format  field is set to a cell array. In R14SP2, if you subscript the Data  field to try to generate a comma-separated list, you instead get back the first element of the list. For example, if you create a memmapfile  object like this in R14SP2,    m=memmapfile(FILENAME, 'format', {'double', 1, 'field1'}, ...                'repeat', 2); and then run   m.data(:).field1 MATLAB assigns only m.data(1).field1  to ans .

Summary: createClassFromWsdl does not handle output arguments correctly
Description: createClassFromWsdl sometimes creates an interface that returns the wrong number of output arguments.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: xpcserialbinarysplit.mdl does not work correctly.
Description: The xPC Target model, xpcserialbinarysplit.mdl , does not work correctly. The constants defining the messages that are sent do not match the constants in the receive binary header block. This prevents the messages from being decoded.
Workaround: As a workaround, if you have a release prior to R14SP3, to send the messages that are being looked for, perform the following modifications:In the MATLAB Command Window, type xpcserialbinarysplitDouble-click the Constant block and change the Constant value parameter to [3 5 170 1]. Double-click the Constant3 block and change the Constant value parameter to [3 170 2 44]. Save and close the model to another name, for example, xpcserialbinarysplit2.mdlUse this model instead of xpcserialbinarysplit.mdl. These changes allow the associated message to be decoded.

Summary: Video Input block displays corrupt strings, preventing models from being saved.
Description: Using the Video Input block with certain image acquisition devices may lead to corrupted strings appearing in the Input Video Format field of the mask, preventing the Simulink model from being saved.

Summary: Model blocks' signal labels are not propagated properly
Description: Simulink reports the following invalid error when a signal entering a Bus Selector block originates from a Model Reference output port that is not explicitly labeled.  Selected signal 'signalLabel' in 'mdl/Bus Selector' is not part of the bus entering the Bus Selector.
Workaround: Label the output signal of the Model block that is connected to a Bus Selector.

Summary: MATLAB errors on Linux when opening print dialog after printing to a file
Description: When running on Linux, MATLAB will print a figure to a file properly the first time, creating a postscript file. However, any subsequent attempts to print from the same figure window raises a java.lang.NullPointerException  error instead of displaying the print dialog.

Summary: Cannot use the Export dialog to export multirate filters from FDATool
Description: You cannot use the File->Export  feature in FDATool to export multirate filters created in the multirate panel.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds.

Summary: Pole/Zero editor slow in FDATool
Description: The pole/zero editor in FDATool is slow.

Summary: Error when generating HDL for a decimating filter with fewer coefficients than decimation factor
Description: In general the Filter Design HDL Coder does not generate HDL for zero-orderFIR filters. If you create a decimating filter where the number ofcoefficients is less than or equal to the decimation rate, the codegeneration process will produce an error similar to the following:??? Error using ==> hdlexpandvectorsignalhdlexpandvectorsignal got a non-vector input input_pipeline_phase0  The error occurs in this case  because each phase in the polyphase decomposition is a zero-order FIR filter.
Workaround: To work around the problem, make sure to have at least two coefficients in the first phase of the polyphase decomposition of the filter. You can check this by calling polyphase on the filter object.

Summary: HDL Filter Link demo does not effectively lowpass filter the signal
Description: The HDL Filter Link demo (hdlfilterlink ) does not work correctly due to a bug in Link for ModelSim. The Link for ModelSim Cosimulation block in Simulink wrongly interprets a block saved from a previous release with no sample time as having an output sample time of 2. This causes the simulation to run with incorrect timing.
Workaround: Open the Link for ModelSim Cosimulation block in the model and set the output sample times to 10 instead of 2.

Summary: To Multimedia File blocks in previously saved models have only one input port
Description: The To Multimedia File block has moved to the Signal Processing Blockset, and the number of input ports is now controlled by the Write  parameter.
Workaround: When you open previously saved models that contain To Multimedia File blocks, you must reconfigure these blocks and reconnect your signal lines.

Summary: Subscripted assignment to logical outputs from Embedded MATLAB Function blocks may cause errors.
Description: An Embedded MATLAB Function block that outputs a logical matrix may cause spurious errors if the output is assigned incrementally.For example, if your output is b , the following type of assignment will generate errors:  b(i) = x;  In this example, x  is any expression.
Workaround: Introduce a temporary variable and assign the result of the final computation to the output. Complete assignments to the output work correctly when they take the form:    b = x;

Summary: isequal always returns "true" for fimath objects
Description: The Fixed-Point Toolbox isequal  function always returns "true" for fimath  objects. For example, the following returns 1  when it should return 0 :a = fimath('roundmode','floor');b = fimath('roundmode','ceil');isequal(a, b)ans =1
Workaround: To work around this problem, use the following syntax instead:isequal(get(a),get(b))ans = 0

Summary: VisionPROBE Initialization Fails When MATLABROOT Path Contains Spaces
Description: The MATLAB Start Menu action to initialize the SingleStep visionPROBE does not deal correctly with spaces in MATLABROOT.
Workaround: To avoid this problem make sure there are no spaces in the path to your MATLAB installation directory.

Summary: Model reference may generate incorrect code
Description: When a referenced model has a continuous rate and a discrete rate, the code generated for model reference may be incorrect. The problem is that the code for the discrete rate does not have a guard to run only in major time steps.  So if the model is referenced from a top model that has continuous states, the discrete rate runs during minor time steps. This leads to incorrect answers.

Summary: Saving and loading database connection objects produces errors
Description: Saving a connection object and trying to load it produces errors and corrupts the MAT-file. For exampleconn = database('Pricing', 'mike', 'bravo');save conn results in a warning that conn  cannot be saved. Loadingconn  also fails.
Workaround: To work around the problem, set the preference for saving MAT-files to-v6. Specifically, in the MATLAB desktop, select File ->Preferences -> General -> MAT-Files. Then select Ensure backwards compatibility (-v6), and click OK. You still cannot save and load the connection object as a variable, however, the MAT-file will not be corrupted.

Summary: Reduced MPC555 performance due to fetch serialization mode (ICTRL register)
Description: The MPC555 ICTRL register is configured to run in "serialize mode". This reduces the performance of the processor in comparison with the alternative non-serialized mode of operation.
Workaround: To configure the MPC555 to run in the higher performance non-serialized mode, follow the steps below:1. Extract "techsolution.zip" (attached below) in to the root of your R14SP2 MATLAB installation (the full path to your MATLAB installation directory, found by entering matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt).Note: you may wish to make backup copies of the original files.2. Rebuild the bootcode by rebuilding the MPC555 driver library and install the new bootcode.Note: applications will not need to be rebuilt; installing the new bootcode will be sufficient.It is important to note the following warning about On-Board External RAM Chip Wait States:An application may fail during run-time if the on-board External RAM chip is not capable of accessing data quickly enough for operation without extra CPU wait states.With this Fetch Serialization performance enhancement, if the model is configured to generate an External RAM application with System Clock running faster than the default 20MHz, this could be fast enough for the application to fail (depending on the type of on-board External RAM chip).The two recommended solutions to this problem are:1) Continue to use the External RAM memory model, but reduce the System Clock to a level at which the on-board External RAM chip can operate with zero CPU wait states.2) Switch the memory model to Internal Flash which will allow maximum performance.

Summary: Build failures caused by spaces in paths
Description: The following build failure will occur if the path to your working directory contains spaces.   This error will also occur if you choose "Rebuild the MPC5xx Driver Library" from the MATLAB Start menu when MATLAB has been installed in a location containing spaces in its path.Build Error Message:Died atD:/MATLAB~1/toolbox/rtw/targets/common/tgtcommon/makedepend.pl line 358.Can't find file exceptions.s whilst processing sourcefile exceptions.s Usage : makedepend.pl -I incpath1 -I incpath2 -I inpath3  D:\MATLAB Install With Spaces\toolbox\rtw\targets\mpc555dk\bin\win32\make.exe: *** [bin/DIAB/555/exceptions.o] Error 2 Error using ==> slbuild Error using ==> RTW.makertw.make_rtw Error using ==> rtw_c Error(s) encountered while building model "Target_IO" >>
Workaround: Please always use a working directory that does not contain spaces in its path.

Summary: Results from the robustfit function may be different
Description: The algorithm for robust fitting involves computing a robust estimate of sigma , the standard deviation of the residuals.  This is computed based on the deviation of the residuals from their median.  In cases where the median residual is substantially different from zero, the results can be undesirable.

Summary: Documentation: clarification needed on use of build subsystem feature with virtual versus nonvirtual subsystems
Description: In the "Building Subsystems and Working with Reference Models" section of the Real-Time Workshop documentation, a recommendation is to add information on always using atomic subsystems in models for which you intend to 'right-click' build to generate code. It should be noted that if you use the Real-Time Workshop Build Subsystem feature (right-click or Tools  menu) for a virtual subsystem, the effect is to treat the subsystem as an atomic subsystem which can change model behavior if you then replace the subsystem in the model with the subsystem's generated code. For example, the model may now contain unexpected algebraic loops.

Summary: Subsystems: Right-click subsystem build mechanism does not preserve virtual or nonvirtual attribute of bus crossing subsystem boundary
Description: The right-click subsystem build mechanism can fail to preserve the virtual or nonvirtual attribute of a bus crossing the boundary of the subsystem you are building. This can lead to unexpected results. For example, an error can result when a nonvirtual bus with a storage class other than Auto  crosses the subsystem boundary.
Workaround: Generate code for the subsystem without specifying a bus object in both the upstream Bus Creator block and the subsystem Inport block.

Summary: TLC STRING directive: crashes when given a vector data structure
Description: The TLC STRING  directive can cause MATLAB to crash when specified with an unexpected data type, such as vector.
Workaround: There are no workarounds.

Summary: FileDependencies might cause warnings in worker log
Description: Use of the FileDependencies  property might cause warning messages to be written to the log of an MDCE worker. This does not impact the execution of tasks by the worker.

Summary: patternsearch sometimes removes linear constraints even if they are consistent and nondegenerate
Description: patternsearch  sometimes removes my linear constraints even if they are consistent and nondegenerate.A code example showing this is:f = [0;0;0;0;0;-1];A = [0 1.598e-007 5.511e-007 2.244e-007 4.865e-007 0]; b = 1.074; Aeq = [0 -1 -1 -1 -1 10216]; beq = 0; lb = zeros(6,1);ub = [1; Inf; Inf; Inf; Inf; Inf];options = psoptimset('Display','none','MaxIter',1e6);[x,fval] = patternsearch(@(x) -x(6),zeros(6,1),A,b,Aeq,beq,lb,ub,options);ConstrTol = Aeq*x-beq This code produces the result:ConstrTol =   Inf When ConstrTol  should actually be near zero according to the equality constraints defined for Aeq  and beq .

Summary: Spurious Real-Time Workshop warning
Description: When loading a model that specifies a custom target file on a system that lacks a Real-Time Workshop license, Simulink may display a warning message that supporting files for the target are missing. Simulink should display the warning only if you have a Real-Time Workshop license.

Summary: Assigning large values to Embedded MATLAB Function Block parameters caused parse errors
Description: Assigning a large value ˇŞ for example, a 100x100 matrix ˇŞ to an Embedded MATLAB Function Block parameter generated the following error:  Parse: This statement is incomplete.
Workaround: Suppose the name of the parameter is mdash. The workaround is as follows:Change the scope of the Embedded MATLAB Function block data object mdash from Parameter to Input using the Model Explorer.The object mdash now appears as an input port to the Embedded MATLAB Function block.Create a new constant block in the Simulink subsystem containing the Embedded MATLAB Function block and specify the constant value in the block properties dialog box as mdash.Connect the output of this block to the input port mdash that you just created in the Embedded MATLAB Function block.

Summary: Reporting a long error message causes Simulink to crash
Description: Reporting a long error message at run time causes Simulink and MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Data Store Read/Write blocks: external IO generated when Data Store Memory block is outside the subsystem targeted for code generation
Description: If all of the following conditions exist, Real-Time Workshop generates inputs and outputs for Data Store Read/Write blocks that are not needed in the generated code and consume global memory: You select a subsystem for code generation with the right-click Build Subsystem  option. The subsystem includes Data Store Read/Write blocks. A parent system contains the corresponding Data Store Memory block.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: dicomwrite errors when using WritePrivate option
Description: The dicomwrite  function, when used with the WritePrivate  option to create a DICOM file with private metadata fields, outputs the following error:   ??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.

Summary: Running MATLAB as Automation server requires administrative privileges
Description: If you start MATLAB on Windows as an Automation server, using the /automation  argument, MATLAB tries to update the Windows registry. This operation requires administrative privileges. If you do not have sufficient privileges, an error results. Excel Link runs MATLAB as an Automation server. If you do not have sufficient privileges to edit the registry, you receive an error like the following when you start Excel Link:Unable to set Registry Value for CLSID\{2A021682-B0D6-4734-BCBF-F8FF3A987E65}. You may not have sufficient privileges.  Re-run MATLAB as a user with Administrator access.

Summary: waitForState and dfeval functions do not work on Macintosh
Description: The waitForState  function waits indefinitely on the Macintosh platform.  This will cause some demonstration programs to fail.   Because the dfeval  function uses waitForState ,this also does not work on a Macintosh.
Workaround: As an alternative to dfeval, you can use dfevalasync.To work around other waitForState issues, try to use the other event mechanisms in your programs instead of waitForState. For example, use the FinishedFcn property on your tasks and jobs.Though not recommended, if necessary, you can circumvent this problem by using a loop.  For example, replace  waitForState(job, 'finished') with   while(1)         pause(5);         if strcmp(job.State,'finished')                 break;         end  end

Summary: Worker hangs when run on Macintosh as root or from the display
Description: An MDCE worker sessions hangs when run on a Macintosh system as root, the console user, or from a display. The worker session starts normally, but hangs when it begins to execute a task.
Workaround: To work around this issue: Shut down all MDCE entities on the machine with     mdce stopDelete all the log and checkpoint files associated with the MDCE entities on this machine.Add a file called java.opts to the directory MATLABROOT/toolbox/distcomp/bin.Edit this new java.opts file to include the following line:    -Djava.awt.headless=trueSave the java.opts file.Restart the MDCE services (mdce, job manager, worker) that you want running on this machine.

Summary: Macintosh: ver function displays incorrect output for MATLAB Compiler
Description: On the Macintosh platform, when you run the ver  function, the list of installed products includes the MATLAB Compiler, even though it is not available on the Macintosh for these releases.

Summary: Stateflow generated macros do not follow ERT code templates
Description: Macros (#define ) that are generated by Stateflow for Stateflow data/states are not placed in the generated code (model.c ) according to ERT code templates. Instead, Stateflow places these #define 's just above a function definition (either a model _step  or Stateflow function).

Summary: Sample-time-independent models cannot access global data stores
Description: Sample-time-independent models cannot access global data stores (see "Working with Data Stores" in the Simulink documentation). This means that a model referenced within a triggered subsystem in a parent model cannot access global data stores.

Summary: Database Toolbox: Using 'javaaddpath' fails to add classes to javaclasspath
Description: Adding classes to the MATLAB javaclasspath using 'javaaddpath' does not expose them to the Database Toolbox. Users can add classes only via classpath.txt.
Workaround: The workaround is to statically add classes to the MATLAB javaclasspath using the classpath.txt file.

Summary: FileDependencies property cannot be set to empty value
Description: You cannot set the value of a job's FileDependencies  property to an empty value ({} ) or an empty string ({''} ).

Summary: Simulink autoscaling cannot be used with models from Filter Wizard
Description: The sum, gain, and convert blocks used in generating a filter with the realizemdl  command or with the Filter Wizard GUI cannot use  Simulink autoscaling. These blocks have their 'LockScale' property 'on'.

Summary: Signal Specification block fails to determine inherited sample time
Description: A Signal Specification block can fail to determine the sample time inherited by the block connected to it if a block downstream from the Signal Specification block has a faster sample time. In this case, the block that drives the Signal Specification block inherits the sample time of the faster downstream block.

Summary: Discrete Integrator block: code generation for data type sizes other than 8, 16, or 32 bits not supported
Description: Real-Time Workshop does not support code generation for Discrete Integrator blocks that use ports with a data type size other than 8, 16, or 32 bits. However, simulation works for other data types. Code generation for Discrete Integrator blocks that use ports of other data type sizes will be supported in a future release.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Array Editor truncates row numbers above 99 on Macintosh and Linux
Description: The Array Editor on Macintosh and Linux platforms sometimes truncates row numbers greater than 99. The row numbers are shown in the left-hand column as "1..", "2..", etc.
Workaround: Use a different font in the Array Editor.

Summary: Flawed sample time optimization involving enabled subsystems
Description: As an optimization, Simulink assigns a constant (inf) sample time to an enabled subsystems that has an unconnected enable port to indicate that the subsystem never runs. This optimization has the following flaws: Downstream blocks do not see initial conditions on the output ports of the subsystem. This leads to incorrect simualtion results. The optimization violates the expected relationship between the sample time of a subsystem and the sample times of its contents. The optimization is inconsistent with the way Simulink treats  grounded enabled subsystems.

Summary: Documentation: References to rtwgen in Target Language Compiler documentation
Description: The Real-Time Workshop Target Language Compiler documentation for R14SP2 and earlier recommends using the rtwgen  and tlc  commands together to create targets and generate code.  The rtwgen  command is not intended for direct use, and upgrading Real Time Workshop may cause code that uses the command to fail.
Workaround: To build executables and model targets, use the following command instead of rtwgen:slbuild('model'[,'TargetType'])

Summary: sprintf/fprintf Return Partial String with Empty Array Input Argument
Description: In release R14, the following commands return only those string input arguments that precede the empty array ([]) argument. This was a regression from release R13. In R14, these examples return just the character 'a'. In R14SP3, they return the string 'abc':   sprintf('%s','a',[],'b','c')  fprintf(fid,'%s','a',[],'b','c')   Furthermore, in R14 and previous releases, the following commands return only those numeric input arguments that precede the empty array ([]) argument. In R14 and previous releases, these examples return just the character '1'. In R14SP3, they return the string '123':   sprintf('%d',1,[],2,3)  fprintf(fid,'%d',1,[],2,3)

Summary: Highlighting items with requirements displays an inconsistent state
Description: The Highlight Items with Requirements  button on the Model Explorer shows an inconsistent state when you toggle between multiple models.

Summary: Subsystems: Real-Time Workshop generates empty terminate functions
Description: In some cases, Real Time Workshop can generate empty terminate functions for subsystems.

Summary: Data Store Read blocks: inefficient code generation when element selected from block vector
Description: If you use a Selector block to select an element from a vector specified for a Data Store Read block, Real-Time Workshop generates an extra temporary copy of the Data Store Memory vector during code generation. This is inefficient in terms of stack allocation.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Editor/Debugger Error When Opening or Saving File on Windows
Description: Opening or saving a file in the Editor/Debugger on Windows systems sometimes results in an error like the following: java.io.FileNotFoundException: ...\Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R14\matlab.prf (Access is denied)at java.io.FileOutputStream.open(Native Method)at java.io.FileOutputStream.(Unknown Source)at java.io.FileOutputStream.(Unknown Source)at com.mathworks.services.Prefs.save(Prefs.java:376)at com.mathworks.services.Prefs$SavePrefsThread.run(Prefs.java:820) This usually occurs when you use folder redirection or work on a common shared desktop. In this situation, MATLAB looks in the wrong directory for user's preferences. The MATLAB Java code locates the preference directory by obtaining the user.home  Java system property and appending \\Application Data\\MathWorks\\MATLAB\\R14 .
Workaround: To work around the problemIn MATLAB, run prefdir. MATLAB returns the location for your preferences directory, for exampleC:\mydirectory\Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R14\matlab.prfCreate a java.opts file and save it to your MATLAB startup directory. (If you need more information about the MATLAB startup directory, in MATLAB, run docsearch('startup directory')).Include the following line in the java.opts file to set the user.home system property:-Duser.home=partialpath to matlab.prfwhere partialpath to matlab.prf is the component preceding \Application Data\MathWorks\MATLAB\R14\matlab.prf returned by the prefdir function you ran in step 2. For the example shown, the entry the java.opts file would be-Duser.home=C:\mydirectory

Summary: Using a DV camera with the Video Input block causes MATLAB to hang.
Description: The Image Acquisition Video Input block does not currently support DV camcorders. Attempting to access the Image Acquisition block library while a DV camcorder is connected to your system will cause MATLAB to hang.

Summary: Running the mapexreg demo on the Macintosh
Description: The demo mapexreg  (Georeferencing an Image to an Orthotile Base Layer) fails on the Macintosh platform because the cpselect  Image Processing Toolbox GUI is not currently available on the Macintosh platform.
