Summary: MATLAB may not call a previously accessible file after !mv
Description: If you have a MATLAB code file in your work directory that calls another MATLAB code file in the private directory, and then you move the private file up one level using the !mv command, the file in your work directory will fail.If file test1.m is in your work directory and calls file test2.m in your private directory, MATLAB creates p-code when test1 is run that points to private/test2.m. If you now move test2.m up one level with the command !mv private/test2.m, then running test1 fails with the error     ??? Previously accessible file private\test2.m is now inaccessible.
Workaround: This has been fixed in release R14sp3. If you choose not to upgrade to this release, you can work around this problem by clearing test1 from memory to delete the old P-code. Use the command    clear test1

Summary: Uicontrol and dialog box text is truncated at the top
Description: Text in a uicontrol or dialog box appears truncated at the top. One example of when this can happen is if you have Adobe software installed that uses Adobe Helvetica fonts.
Workaround: Change the default UIcontrol font with this command:set(0,'DefaultUicontrolFontname','Sans Serif'); This changes the default font for all UI controls.You can include this statement in your startup.m file to ensure that it is executed in every MATLAB session. For more information on creating a startup.m file, execute the following command in the MATLAB Command Window:doc startup

Summary: Startup on PC fails and MATLAB terminates
Description: MATLAB fails to start on a PC. The stack trace message that appears in the Command Window starts with this line: [0] m_interpreter.dll:public: bool __thiscall dbstopIfTable::getIdentifier
Workaround: The problem is bad data in a matlab.ini file created by a previous version of MATLAB that ran on the same PC. To fix the problem, delete the c:\WINNT\matlab.ini file.

Summary: Macintosh: GUIs with pop-up menus exhibit problematic behavior
Description: On the Macintosh, GUIs that have pop-up menu uicontrols may show two behaviors:The pop-up menu flashes intermittently. CPU utilization may go up to 100%.
Workaround: In many cases you can work around this problem by placing the pop-up menu inside a uipanel as in the following example:figureh = uipanel;h1=uicontrol('Parent',h,...      String',{'One','Two'},...      'Style','popupmenu');Some users have reported that terminating X11 before starting MATLAB also resolves the problem.You may also use the attached utility fixpopupuicontrol to work around this problem. This utility takes a figure handle as an argument and reparents all the pop-up menu uicontrols to uipanels. Please note that this utility may not work in GUIs if the UI code is dependent on a specific parent-child hierarchy.If you are experiencing this problem while using MATLAB in nojvm mode, it is advised to start MATLAB with the JVM on. Starting MATLAB in no JVM mode restricts functionality. With nojvm, you cannot use the MATLAB desktop, or any tools that require Java.

Summary: Measurement Computing PCI-DAS64/M1/16, PCI-DAS64/M2/16, and PCI-DAS64/M3/16 Cards Acquisition Fixed
Description: The Data Acquisition Toolbox does not always acquire data using the Measurement Computing PCI-DAS64/M1/16, PCI-DAS64/M2/16, and PCI-DAS64/M3/16 cards.
Workaround: You need to reboot your system when attempting to acquire data using the Measurement Computing PCI-DAS64/M1/16, PCI-DAS64/M2/16, and PCI-DAS64/M3/16 cards.

Summary: Why do I receive the error: Undefined function or variable "initdesktoputils" when starting MATLAB?
Description: This error can occur when the MATLAB Component Runtime (MCR) is installed on the development machine. It is caused by MATLAB Compiler-generated applications sharing MATLAB's preferences directory.
Workaround: If you are experiencing the problem in R14SP2 or earlier, you can resolve it by typing:>> rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Calling MATLAB Compiler-generated DLL from JNI causes segmentation violations
Description: MATLAB Compiler-generated shared libraries or DLLs cannot be called from Java applications via the JNI interface (or any other interface, for that matter). This problem is caused because the MATLAB Compiler-generated shared libraries and DLLs start a Java virtual machine (JVM) on initialization. If the process already contains a JVM (as all Java applications will), starting the second JVM will fail, and the Compiler-generated component will not initialize properly.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Blocks incur delay during simulation
Description: All RF Blockset physical blocks incur a modeling delay during simulation. You must take this delay into consideration especially when using these blocks in feedback configurations.

Summary: MATLAB worker checks out a Simulink license when running rsim executables
Description: MATLAB workers check out a Simulink license when running RSim executables.

Summary: Some Concerto text files cannot be loaded
Description: Concerto text files that do not end their first line with a tab character cannot be imported into the Model Browser's Data Editor.

Summary: Rapid Simulation Target: Transport Delay block requires fixed size buffers
Description: When using a Transport Delay block with a Rapid Simulation (RSim) target, you must use the block with fixed size buffers. This restriction is not required when using the block with the Simulink Accelerator.

Summary: DM642 EVM Video ADC and DAC blocks exhibit video artifacts including fragmentation and jitter in moving objects
Description: DM642 EVM video ADC and DAC blocks use a TI video port driver to capture and display NTSC video streams. In versions released earlier than R14SP3, the DM642 EVM used an interrupt service routine (ISR)-based task scheduler that was making calls to TI's video port driver. This caused video artifacts such as making moving objects appear jittery or fragmented on the video monitor.The TI video port driver is intended to be used within a TSK thread as it includes implicit semaphore calls inside the driver. When used in the context of a hardware interrupt service routine (ISR), the video port driver does not work correctly, causing the artifacts described above.

Summary: LCC Compiler ignores MCC output directory (-d) when building shared libraries
Description: If you compile a shared library using the LCC compiler and use the -d  option to direct the files to a specified directory, the .lib  file does not get copied to the specified output directory.
Workaround: This is a bug in the LCC compiler. Currently there are no known workarounds for this issue. The lib files need to be seperately copied to the output directory when using the -d option with MCC while using the LCC compiler on Win32.

Summary: Event handling not working
Description: In R14 event handling did not work for MATLAB Builder for COM components. Consequently, calls to M-functions containing the %#event pragma were not properly dispatched to the Visual Basic callback implementation. The MCR issues callbacks on a thread different from the caller's thread, and Visual Basic does not support the execution of callbacks on any thread other than the main thread of the application.

Summary: Problems with To Video Display blocks in executables generated by Real-Time Workshop
Description: Real-Time Workshop generated executables are not Windows based applications with a message queue. As a result, when Windows XP attempts toverify that the executable is running, the operating system does not receive a response. Therefore, Windows XP concludes that there is a problem with the executable, and it replaces the top-level window with a Ghost Window, which is an empty black or white window.
Workaround: The following Microsoft Knowledge Base Article describes how to disable the Ghost Window feature:  http://support.microsoft.com/default.aspx?scid=kb;en-us;820252&Product=winxp

Summary: fitsread takes a long time to read large bintable datasets
Description: fitsread  takes a long time to read large binary table (bintable) datasets.

Summary: The Uncertain Parameters Dialog Does Not Work on Macintosh
pause Function Sometimes Fails on UNIX and Macintosh Platforms
Description: The pause  function does not always stop at the expected line in a MATLAB file. That is, sometimes lines after the pause  statement execute before MATLAB pauses.
Workaround: Start MATLAB using the -nojvm startup option. For details, view the relevant documentation, which you can access from MATLAB by runningdocsearch('nojvm startup option unix')

Summary: Setting the legend EdgeColor to none can cause a Segmentation Violation
Description: Setting the EdgeColor  property of a legend object to 'None'  can make MATLAB hang or result in a segmentation violation. For example,  surf(peaks);  h = legend('test');  set(h,'EdgeColor','None'); triggers this problem.
Workaround: Set the legend Box property to 'Off' instead of setting the EdgeColor to 'None'.

Summary: Simulator flags option not functional
Description: The Simulator flags  option on the TestBench Options  dialog (and the SimulatorFlags  property)are not yet fully functional. They are included in the product interface for future use.

Summary: Rapid Simulation Target: C-API does not work with target
Description: Limitations in the Rapid Simulation (RSim) target code prevent you from using the target with Real-Time Workshop C-API support.

Summary: Internal memory map option on the LF2407 eZdsp is not supported
Description: Selecting Internal_memory_map  in the Target Preferences blockfor LF2407 eZdsp board generates a Full_memory_map  linker command file. This is because Internal_memory_map  is currently not supported for the LF2407 eZdsp board.

Summary: MPC56x Internal RAM (CALRAM) overlay memory is not configured for use with the Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555
Description: The default memory model does not use the full 36k of internal RAM available on the MPC565/MPC566.Similarly, the full amount of internal RAM on the MPC561-4 is not mapped with the default memory model.
Workaround: You can increase IRAM to the 36k limit of internal RAM on the MPC565 as follows (adjust as appropriate for other variants):1. Modify "ram_len" to "0x9000" in$MATLAB/toolbox/rtw/targets/mpc555dk/drivers/make_plugins/mpc555_build_rules/memlayout.565.prop (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typingmatlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.)2. Rebuild the MPC555 drivers and install the new bootcode as documented in the Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 help3. Rebuild the application.

Summary: Several Microsoft ActiveX controls do not display properly.
Description: A number of Microsoft ActiveX controls do not appear correctly in MATLAB. Some controls are created correctly, but the text fields are displayed wrong. The following is a partial list of PROGID's or names of the controls that had problems in R14SP2.MSCOMctlLib.sbarCtrl.2AMOVIE.ActiveMovieControl.2Microsoft TabStrip Control, version 6.0 wmplayer.ocxNetmeetingApplicationOdometer ActiveX control mscomctllib.treectrliead32.AXReader.1AMOVIE.ActiveMovieControl.2MSComctlLib.ListViewCtrl.2Windows Media Player ActiveX control Forms.TextBox.1Forms.CheckBox.1Forms.CommandButton.1Forms.Image.1Forms.OptionButton.1Forms.ScrollBar.1Forms.SpinButton.1Forms.TabStrip.1Forms.ToggleButton.1MSCOMctlLib.sbarCtrl.2

Summary: Why does the print command in a deployed application ignore figure properties such as PaperPosition?
Description: The print  command in a deployed application resizes and orients the printed figure so as to use as much of the printed page as possible. It does this despite any settings of figure properties such as PaperPostionMode , Position , PaperPosition , PaperSize , and PaperUnits .
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Simulation stop time not honored for multitasking models using ERT mode
Description: The Simulink simulation stop time is not honored in code generated for a multitasking model, ERT-based target when the -o2 optimization is used.
Workaround: You can fix this problem by using the Stop Simulation block inside your model.

Summary: Rapid Simulation Target: incorrect results produced for non-inlined S-functions with variable sample time
Description: The Rapid Simulation (RSim) target does not properly handle S-functions that  Have a variable sample time Do not have a TLC file for code generation  The S-functions are not called at the correct time, which leads to computations that produce incorrect answers.

Summary: depfun can now output eval_strings
Description: The 6th output argument of the depfun  function now contains a cell array of the files in TRACE_LIST that call eval  (or evalin , feval , fevalc , or evalc ). The strings that eval  and similar functions evaluate might use functions that are not in TRACE_LIST. Please note that depfun  can't always list all the dependent functions of an m/p-file.  This 6th output argument can help you find some of the gaps.

Summary: Holes in polygons appear filled-in
Description: Polygons with islands (inner, hollow rings) can be represented inversion 2 geographic data structures (geostruct2s) and can be readfrom polygon shapefiles. Such polygons should display their inner ringsso that they look like "holes" (inner, unfilled polygons that do not obscure underlying features or objects), but currently the holes are filled by display functions geoshow , mapview , and mapshow . The boundaries of the islands are visible if the EdgeColor  and FaceColor  properties differ, but the island interiors are filled with the FaceColor .

Summary: Japanese directories: some names prevent access on Japanese Windows systems
Description: Some Japanese characters encoded in Shift-JIS have the 0x5C (decimal value: 92) character code as the second byte of a multi-byte character. If a directory name ends with such a multi-byte character, you cannot access the directory. If you try to, MATLAB displays a directory access failure message in the Command Window. See examples of these characters in the GIF file in the attached ZIP file.
Workaround: As a workaround, rename the directory.

Summary: Array Editor memory limitation with certain large arrays
Description: When using the Array Editor to view a matrix with a large number of rows or columns (but with fewer than the documented limit of 2^19 elements), an error message appears on the command line:  "java.lang.OutOfMemoryError"; MATLAB then slows down and may subsequently quit without notice. No indication is provided that the matrix is too large to be viewed in the Array Editor, but in fact it is.
Workaround: The limit of array size supported by the Array Editor to 2^19 elements is necessary, but not sufficient, to avoid out-of-memory problems. Such problems also occur when more than about 2^16 rows or columns are present.  To avoid this situation, avoid attempting to open arrays that have more than 2^16 rows or columns.

Summary: Function inputs and outputs are renamed in Model Explorer
Description: In previous Model Explorer releases, function inputs andoutputs had a scope of Function input  or Function output . The narrow width of the display column made it difficult to differentiate between the two. These scopes have been renamed to Input  and Output  respectively. Some script-based interactions with Stateflow.Data  objects that rely on the old names might not work anymore. In particular, the following will no longer find data because there is no data with that scope name.  sfr = sfroot;inputData = sfr.find('-isa','Stateflow.Data','Scope','Function input'); The following is also not a suitable replacement because it will also find chart inputs. sfr = sfroot;inputData = sfr.find('-isa','Stateflow.Data','Scope','Input');
Workaround: As a workaround for existing script-based interactions, the following code should provide the correct result:sfr = sfroot;functions = sfr.find('-isa','Stateflow.Function');inputData = functions.find('-isa','Stateflow.Data','Scope','Input');

Summary: Analog phase modulation blocks are incompatible with Real-Time Workshop
Description: The PM Modulator Passband and PM Demodulator Passband blocks do not support code generation with Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: hdldaemon shutdown in shared memory mode on Windows requires exiting ModelSim simulator
Description: If your application uses shared memory communication on a Windows platform and you try to use hdldaemon  to shut down the MATLAB server while a ModelSim connection exists, the shutdown fails and a message similar to the following appears:    ??? HDLDaemon server shutdown FAILED.    Make sure all connections from ModelSim are closed.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, close a connection from ModelSim by doing one of the following:o Close the connection without stopping the ModelSim simulation by entering the nomatlabtb command.o Stop the ModelSim simulation by entering the quit -sim command.o Exit ModelSim entirely by entering the quit command.

Summary: CCSINSPECT demo leaves dangling handles in the MATLAB workspace
Description: A bug in the function inspect  causes MATLAB not to release an open instance of a ccsdsp  object. The open object (a dangling handle) generates an error when you try to connect to a different processor or target using a new object.To demonstrate the problem, perform the following steps:Set-up CCS on a certain processor (such as the C67x).Run ccsinspect .Load a program and highlight any symbol.Click Inspect Properties .Close the ccsinspect  demo.Type clear all  in MATLAB. This should delete all variables in your MATLAB workspace.Set up CCS for a different processor, such as the C64x.Type cc=ccsdsp  in MATLAB. This generates an error saying  ??? Error using ==> ccsdsp Dangling Handle(s) - Clear all CCSDSP links and reconnect  The error indicates that an instance of your ccsdsp  object remains in the MATLAB workspace. In this case, the ccsinspect  demo, through Inspect Properties , did not release an instance of your ccsdsp  object.
Workaround: To release this handle and get rid of this error message, exit and restart MATLAB, and then reconnect to the target.

Summary: dicomwrite fails when reading certain numeric private metadata
Description: The dicomwrite  function fails when copying certain files with private values that must be stored as a "decimal string" or "integer string."  There are some private metadata values that dicomwrite  cannot write and others that it can write but which produce the following error when given private metadata that is numeric but not UINT8: ??? Error using ==> dicom_add_attr (validate_data)Attribute (0021,1011) has wrong data type. The data values that cause this error arise when either a user edits the private metadata, or dicominfo  is used on a file with explicit VR and the VR is for a numeric type (such as DS, IS, SS).  If the private attribute is not in a custom data dictionary, dicomwrite  cannot tell what the right output type is.

Summary: VHDL ports of type ARRAY(0 to 0) not supported
Description: Currently, Link for ModelSim does not support VHDL ports of type ARRAY(0 to 0). For example, you cannot use the following type definition:TYPE fake_bool is ARRAY (0 to 0) of std_logic   Use of a port of this type causes ModelSim to crash, potentially leaving MATLAB and the communication link in a bad or inconsistent state.Support for this type definition will be added in a future release of the product.

Summary: exist may not find pathnames that use "." or ".."
Description: The exist function may not find pathnames that use "." or "..". For example, assume that toolbox/simulink/simdemos/aerospace is on your search path. Then exist('f14.mdl') or exist('f14') or exist('aerospace/f14.mdl') all return 4, meaning that the MDL-file is on the MATLAB search path. But if you change directories (cd), and then try to use exist with '.' or '..' in the filename, exist returns either 2 or 0, depending on whether the ls command was able to find the file.    cd('[matlabroot '/toolbox/simulink/simdemos'])    exist ../simdemos/aerospace/f14.mdl % ans = 2    exist ../simdemos/aerospace/f14 % ans = 0   Note that this example uses an MDL-file to make the symptoms clearer. exist should return 4, and may return 2 or 0. If you are looking for a MATLAB file, then you can expect exist to return 2, and may see 0.
Workaround: If you do not upgrade to R14SP3, then you should either use no directory in the filename, or else use a fully qualified filename. For example,cd('[matlabroot '/toolbox/simulink/simdemos']) exist([pwd '/aerospace/f14'])

Summary: Axis position wrong with perspective projection
Description: If the camera is placed inside a 3d Axes and the axes 'projection' is set to 'perspective', then many things draw incorrectly.% run the followingx1=[3368 4500];y1=[0 0];z1=[-3548 -3548];x2=[0 3355];y2=[0 0];z2=[-3545 -3048];f=figure;axis off;l1=line('xdata',x1,'ydata',y1,'zdata',z1,'color',[1 0 0]);l2=line('xdata',x2,'ydata',y2,'zdata',z2,'color',[0 0 1]);set(gcf,'renderer','zbuff');set(gca,'projection','perspective');set(gca,'cameraposition',[3631.88 -53.7942 -3600.13]);set(gca,'cameratarget',[3355.14 0 -3545.2]);set(gca,'cameraviewangle',20);set(gca,'dataaspectratio',[1 1 1]);set(gca,'plotboxaspectratio',[2.5 2 1]); At this point, nothing draws and the Position  property returns the following: get(gca,'Position')[0.1732    0.1100 -186.4625 -107.2100] However, the following line fixes this problem:get(gca) Now the position is correct get(gca,'Position') [0.1300    0.1100    0.7750    0.8150]
Workaround: Issue the following command before querying the Position property:get(gca)See Bug description for information.

Summary: Documentation: description of LibSystemOutputCustomCode() in Target Language Compiler documentation needs to be updated
Description: The description of the LibSystemOutputCustomCode()  function in the Target Language Compiler documentation needs to be updated. The current description defines the "header"  value for the location  argument as follows: "to place buffer at top of function" The description of "header"  needs to be updated as follows: "to place buffer at top of function, before any execution code"

Summary: Inputs on National Instruments PCI-4472, PCI-4474, PXI-4472, and PXI-4474 boards are scaled incorrectly
Description: The signal data acquired using the National Instruments PCI-4472, PCI-4474, PXI-4472, and PXI-4474 appears to be one half the expected amplitude. Note that there is no saturation; it is only the scaling that is changed.
Workaround: Configure all the channels to scale the acquired signal by 2 to get the correct reading.  After creating your analoginput object and using addchannel to add all the channels, execute the following:set(ai.Channel(:),'UnitsRange',[-20 20])Note that this workaround takes advantage of all 23 bits avaliable on this device, without a loss of resolution.  This device consumes one bit in its internal digital calibration routine.

Summary: Look-Up Table (2-D) block: block does not support changing number of repeated zeros/points
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generates code for Look-Up Table (2-D) or Look-Up Table (1-D) blocks with the following block parameter settings, the resulting generated code explicitly includes the number and position of repeated zeros. Floating-point parameters with binary search Extrapolation Linear interpolation  If you then tune the breakpoint parameters, for example, by changing the monotonicity and the number or position of repeated zeros changes, in most cases, the generated code gets an incorrect answer when the input for the breakpoint you set is near zero.
Workaround: Do not change the number or position of repeated zeros in breakpoint parameters for Look-Up Table (2-D) or Look-Up Table (1-D) blocks.

Summary: imresize function has some subtle inconsistencies in how it scales images
Description: The imresize  function does not compute the scaling factor the same way for all of the interpolation methods.  Also, the horizontal and vertical scale factors are sometimes slightly different when rescaling rectangular images.

Summary: Digital I/O objects cannot be restarted
Description: Under some conditions, a digital I/O object cannot be started a second time, preventing the use of TimerFcn callbacks with digital I/O objects.

Summary: mat2str Does Not Preserve Class of Nondouble Inputs
Description: mat2str   does not preserve the class of the input value. The output string always defaults to double  .

Summary: Reading multiple DTED tiles that span 50 degrees N/S latitude
Description: Function dted  cannot handle a latitude range spanning 50 degrees (north or south). At these latitudes, the longitude sampling decreases as the meridians converge toward the poles. In DTED Level 0, for example, data cells change in size from 30 seconds square to 30 seconds in latitude by 60 seconds in longitude. When a set of tiles that cross 50 degrees N/S are read, the following error arises:??? Error using ==> vertcat All rows in the bracketed expression must have the same number of columns. Error in ==> dted>dtedc at 272 tmap = [tmap; map{j}]; Error in ==> dted at 65   [map,maplegend,UHL,DSI,ACC] = dtedc(varargin{:});

Summary: PostSaveFcn Cannot Find Model on First Save
Description: The first time you save a new model that has PostSaveFcn  functions, the PostSaveFcn  functions cannot find the model.

Summary: Find Files Tool Produces Out of Memory Exception for Extensive Searches
Description: When you selectEdit -> Find Files  to use the Find Files tool, and try to perform an extensive search, such as searching the entire MATLAB path for the word edit , the search might fail and display a Java out of memory error in the Command Window, much like the following:java.lang.OutOfMemoryErrorat java.lang.StringBuffer.append(Compiled Code)at java.io.BufferedReader.readLine(Compiled Code)at com.mathworks.ide.browsers.FindView.grepFile(Compiled Code)at com.mathworks.ide.browsers.FindView.findInFiles(Compiled Code)at com.mathworks.ide.widgets.MWReplaceDialog$FindInFilesThread.doFind(Compiled Code)at com.mathworks.ide.widgets.MWReplaceDialog$FindInFilesThread.run(Compiled Code)
Workaround: Try to limit the extent of the search, for example, by looking in fewer directories.
